
# ST Differential Expression Analysis

【Purpose】 Identify brain region-specific differentially expressed genes. Investigate the effects of drug treatment on the transcriptome level across different brain regions.
【Method】 For the differential expression gene identification section, use the results from Wilcoxon test, apply filtering, and then perform analysis once from a hypothesis-driven perspective and once from a data-driven perspective. Finally, display the expression patterns of the selected genes/pathways.

## DEGs Identification: wilcox
Use the wilcox method in `Seurat::FindMarkers` for DEGs identification. Compare the number of DEGs obtained under different log2FC thresholds.

```{r eval=FALSE}
source("script/load_checkpoint.r")
deg.outdir <- paste0(save_dir, "003DEGs/")
if (! dir.exists(deg.outdir)) dir.create(deg.outdir)
expr <- get_expression_values(testcombo)
f <- file("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/background.txt", "w")
cat("_BACKGROUND\t", paste(row.names(expr@exprMat), collapse=","), "\n", sep="", file=f)
close(f)
pairs <- list()
pairs[[1]] <- c("PLX5622", "FADCtrl")
pairs[[2]] <- c("FADCtrl", "WTCtrl")
pairs[[3]] <- c("PLX5622", "WTCtrl")
```

### Seurat::FindMarkers
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Original script: script/003identify_DEGs.wilcox.r
#-------------------------#
# Use Seurat::FindMarkers #
#-------------------------#
Giotto2Seurat <- function(obj){
  library(Seurat)
  expr <- get_expression_values(obj)@exprMat
  meta <- as.data.frame(pDataDT(obj))
  row.names(meta) <- meta$cell_ID
  obj.seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts=expr, meta.data=meta)
  obj.seurat <- NormalizeData(obj.seurat)
  return(obj.seurat)
}
# bug: MAST:::checkAssayNames line 131
# if(!isTRUE(all.equal(dn[[1]], fData$primerid))) stop('Order of `exprsArray` and `fData` doesn\'t match')
# Changed to:
# if(!all(dn[[1]] == fData$primerid)) stop('Order of `exprsArray` and `fData` doesn\'t match')
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(testcombo)
source("script/utils/findMarkersEachCell.r")
# Question: Should sample/treatment be added to latent.vars? It is not added here, but it seems to be added on the single-cell side.
# The following function has been updated and should be re-run.
# Different filtering thresholds can be used.
# output: 001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.csv
for (log2fc in seq(0, 1, 0.05)){
  findMarkersEachCell(obj=st.seurat, group_col="treatment", cellType_col="cell_types",
                    pairs=pairs, outdir="001Giotto_out/003DEGs", exclude="C12",
                    test.use="wilcox", log2fc_cutoff=log2fc)
}
# Do not filter using log2fc, output in one-row-one-gene format. This format is easier to filter.
meta <- pDataDT(testcombo)
brain_regions <- unique(meta$cell_types)
brain_regions <- setdiff(brain_regions, "C12")
st.DEGs <- list()
n <- 1
for (x in brain_regions) {
  for (y in c("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_WTCtrl")){
    df <- read.csv(paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", x, ".", y, ".csv"),
                 row.names=1)
    df <- df[df$p_val_adj < 0.05, ]
    df$brain_region <- x
    df$comparison <- y
    df$gene <- row.names(df)
    st.DEGs[[n]] <- df
    n <- n + 1
  }
}
st.DEGs <- do.call(rbind, st.DEGs)
write.csv(st.DEGs, "001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv")
# Giotto::findScranMarkers
dir.create("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox.Giotto")
dir.create("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/t.Giotto")
# Compare between samples in the same BRAIN REGION.
S.column <- pDataDT(testcombo)$treatment
CT.column <- pDataDT(testcombo)$cell_types
testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna@metaDT$treatment_CT <- paste(S.column, CT.column, sep="_")
for(test_type in c("t", "wilcox")){
  if (test_type == "wilcox") next
  for (pair in pairs){
    for (C in unique(CT.column)){
      # C12 only exists in one sample and cannot be used for differential expression analysis, so it needs to be excluded.
      if (C == "C12") next
      S1 <- pair[1]
      S2 <- pair[2]
      scran_markers = findScranMarkers(gobject = testcombo,
                                   cluster_column = 'treatment_CT',
                                   group_1 = paste(S1, C, sep = "_"),
                                   group_2 = paste(S2, C, sep = "_"),
                                   test.type=test_type)
      # BUG in findScranMarkers: This function does not always find markers of group1.
      # It sometimes finds markers of group2. Which one is used depends on the order() function.
      out.df <- scran_markers[[1]]
      if (! all(out.df$cluster == paste(S1, C, sep = "_"))){
        out.df <- scran_markers[[2]]
      }
      out.base <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2, ".", C, ".csv")
      write.csv(out.df, paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/", test_type, ".Giotto/", out.base))
    }
  }
}
# Organize the data.
# The wilcox method does not output summary.logFC, but outputs summary.AUC instead.
header <- c("method", "cellType", "comparison",
            "direction", "numOfGene", "geneList")
for (log2fc in c(0.2, 0.5, 1)){
  c.list <- as.list(1:length(header)) # Used to collect results. One item per column.
  names(c.list) <- header
  n = 1
  for (pair in pairs){
    for (C in unique(CT.column)){
      # C12 only exists in one sample and cannot be used for differential expression analysis, so it needs to be excluded.
      if (C == "C12") next
      S1 <- pair[1]
      S2 <- pair[2]
      out.df.combined <- list()
      out.base <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2, ".", C, ".csv")
      out.df <- read.csv(file.path("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox.Giotto", out.base), row.names=1)
      out.df.combined[["up"]] <- subset(out.df, FDR < 0.05 & summary.logFC > lfc.cutoff)
      out.df.combined[["down"]] <- subset(out.df, FDR < 0.05 & summary.logFC < -lfc.cutoff)
      for (direct in c("up", "down")){
        tmp.df <- out.df.combined[[direct]]
        write.csv(tmp.df, paste0(out.dir, "C_", C, "__", S1,
                                           "_vs_", S2, ".", direct, ".csv"))
        DEGs <- tmp.df$feats
        c.list[["method"]][n] <- "Giotto.wilcox"
        c.list[["cellType"]][n] <- C
        c.list[["comparison"]][n] <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2)
        c.list[["direction"]][n] <- direct
        c.list[["numOfGene"]][n] <- length(DEGs)
        c.list[["geneList"]][n] <- paste(DEGs, collapse = ";")
        n = n + 1
      }
    }
  }
  out.combined <- data.frame(c.list)
  out.path <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox.Giotto/wilcox.each_region.log2fc_",
                     log2fc, ".csv")
  write.csv(out.combined, out.path)
}
# Investigate the consistency between Giotto and Seurat wilcox methods.
library(ggplot2)
DEGs.seurat <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/Olfactory area.PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl.csv", row.names=1)
DEGs.giotto <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox.Giotto/PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl.Olfactory area.csv", row.names=1)
intersect(row.names(DEGs.seurat[1:100, ]), head(DEGs.giotto$feats, 100)) # 100 genes, completely identical.
# Therefore, the wilcox method of Giotto and Seurat should be the same, except that Seurat seems to have performed some post-processing on the scran results.
```

## DEGs Identification: MAST
```{r}
pairs <- list()
pairs[[1]] <- c("PLX5622", "FADCtrl")
pairs[[2]] <- c("FADCtrl", "WTCtrl")
pairs[[3]] <- c("PLX5622", "WTCtrl")
#
# S.column <- pDataDT(testcombo)$treatment
# CT.column <- pDataDT(testcombo)$cell_types
# testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna@metaDT$treatment_CT <- paste(S.column, CT.column, sep="_")
#
# for (pair in pairs){
# for (C in unique(CT.column)){
# # C12 only exists in one sample and cannot be used for differential expression analysis, so it needs to be excluded.
# if (C == "C12") next
# pair <- pairs[[1]]
# C <- "Isocortex"
# S1 <- pair[1]
# S2 <- pair[2]
# markers = findMastMarkers(gobject = testcombo,
# cluster_column = 'treatment_CT',
# group_1 = paste(S1, C, sep = "_"),
# group_2 = paste(S2, C, sep = "_"),
# adjust_columns="batch")
# # Error: Error in MAST::FromMatrix(exprsArray = expr_data, cData = column_data, :
# # `exprsArray` must be matrix, 3-D array, `list` or `SimpleList`
# out.base <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2, ".", C, ".csv")
# write.csv(out.df, paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/", test_type, ".Giotto/", out.base))
# }
# }
Giotto2Seurat <- function(obj){
  library(Seurat)
  expr <- get_expression_values(testcombo)@exprMat
  meta <- as.data.frame(pDataDT(testcombo))
  row.names(meta) <- meta$cell_ID
  obj.seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(counts=expr, meta.data=meta)
  obj.seurat <- NormalizeData(obj.seurat)
  return(obj.seurat)
}
# bug: MAST:::checkAssayNames line 131
# if(!isTRUE(all.equal(dn[[1]], fData$primerid))) stop('Order of `exprsArray` and `fData` doesn\'t match')
# Changed to:
# if(!all(dn[[1]] == fData$primerid)) stop('Order of `exprsArray` and `fData` doesn\'t match')
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(testcombo)
source("script/utils/findMarkersEachCell.r")
# Question: Should sample/treatment be added to latent.vars? It is not added here, but it seems to be added on the single-cell side.
# The following function has been updated and should be re-run.
# Different filtering thresholds can be used.
# output: 001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.csv
for (log2fc in seq(0, 1, 0.05)){
  log2fc <- 0
  findMarkersEachCell(obj=st.seurat, group_col="treatment", cellType_col="cell_types",
                    pairs=pairs, outdir="001Giotto_out/003DEGs", exclude="C12",
                    test.use="MAST", latent.vars="batch", log2fc_cutoff=log2fc)
}
deg.list <- list()
n <- 1
for (pair in pairs){
  for (C in unique(CT.column)){
    # C12 only exists in one sample and cannot be used for differential expression analysis, so it needs to be excluded.
    if (C == "C12") next
    S1 <- pair[1]
    S2 <- pair[2]
    df <- read.csv(paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/MAST/", C, ".", S1, "_vs_", S2, ".csv"), row.names=1)
    df$brain_region <- C
    df$comparison <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2)
    df$gene <- row.names(df)
    deg.list[[n]] <- df
    n <- n + 1
  }
}
deg.df <- Reduce(rbind, deg.list)
deg.df <- subset(deg.df, p_val_adj < 0.05)
write.csv(deg.df, "001Giotto_out/003DEGs/MAST/st.DEGs.mast.csv")
```

### Investigation of Batch Effect Genes (EDA)
```{r}
meta <- data.frame(testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna@metaDT)
row.names(meta) <- meta$cell_ID
gobj.count <- CreateSeuratObject(testcombo@expression$cell$rna$raw@exprMat,
                              meta.data=meta)
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(gobj.count)
summed <- scuttle::aggregateAcrossCells(sce,
             id=colData(sce)[,"list_ID"])
dds <- DESeqDataSet(summed, ~ treatment + batch)
dds <- DESeq(dds)
resultsNames(dds)
res <- results(dds, name = "batch_2_vs_1")
res <- data.frame(res)
res <- na.omit(res)
res <- res[res$padj < 0.05, ]
res <- res[order(res$pvalue), ]
nrow(res)
# [1] 670
res <- results(dds, name = "treatment_PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
res <- data.frame(res)
res <- na.omit(res)
res <- res[res$padj < 0.05, ]
res <- res[order(res$pvalue), ]
nrow(res) # 101
res <- results(dds, name = "treatment_WTCtrl_vs_FADCtrl")
res <- data.frame(res)
res <- na.omit(res)
res <- res[res$padj < 0.05, ]
res <- res[order(res$pvalue), ]
nrow(res) # 210
# https://personal.broadinstitute.org/scalvo/MitoCarta3.0/mouse.mitocarta3.0.html
# http://ribosome.med.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/rpg.cgi?mode=orglist&org=Mus%20musculus
```

### Removal of Ribosomal and Mitochondrial Genes (EDA)
```{r}
library(readxl)
ribo <- read_excel("000data/ribosome.xlsx")
mito <- read_excel("000data/Mouse.MitoCarta3.0.xls")
ribo.gene <- intersect(ribo$gene, row.names(summed))
mito.gene <- intersect(mito$Symbol, row.names(summed))
total.count <- sum(assay(summed))
summed.ribo <- summed[ribo.gene, ]
ribo.count <- sum(assay(summed.ribo))
ribo.count / total.count
# [1] 0.07582495
summed.mito <- summed[mito.gene, ]
mito.count <- sum(assay(summed.mito))
mito.count / total.count
# 0.2492999
summed.sub <- summed[!row.names(summed) %in% c(ribo.gene, mito.gene), ]
dds <- DESeqDataSet(summed.sub, ~ treatment + batch)
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds, name = "treatment_PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
res <- data.frame(res)
res <- na.omit(res)
res <- res[res$padj < 0.05, ]
res <- res[order(res$pvalue), ]
nrow(res)
# 98, did not increase but decreased instead.
setdiff(row.names(res), st.DEGs$gene)
# [1] "Crybb1" "Slc2a5" "Stab1" "Ccl9" "Bin2" "Cd33"
# [7] "Fcgr1" "Alox5ap" "Tnfaip8l2" "Fermt3" "Havcr2" "Ang"
# [13] "Lcp1" "Slc13a3" "Phyhd1" "Spi1"
normalized.count <- counts(dds, normalized=T)
normalized.count["Ripk1", ]
# FADCtrl_1 FADCtrl_2 PLX5622_1 PLX5622_2 WTCtrl_1 WTCtrl_2
# 182.5153 188.6071 191.3566 152.5518 203.0862 141.0651
```

#---
## Spot DEGs Statistics
### Statistics of DEGs by Brain Region
```{r}
st.DEGs <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.agg <- aggregate(gene~brain_region+comparison, FUN=length, data=st.DEGs)
st.DEGs.agg <- st.DEGs.agg[order(st.DEGs.agg$comparison, st.DEGs.agg$gene), ]
write.csv(st.DEGs.agg, "001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.agg.csv")
```

### Comparison of Several DEGs Identification Methods
```{r}
st.DEGs.wilcox <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.mast <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/MAST/st.DEGs.mast.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.deseq <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/DESeq/st.DEGs.deseq.csv", row.names=1)
a <- subset(st.DEGs.wilcox, brain_region == "Hippocampus" & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" & avg_log2FC > 0, select="gene", drop=T)
b <- subset(st.DEGs.mast, brain_region == "Hippocampus" & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" & avg_log2FC > 0, select="gene", drop=T)
c <- subset(st.DEGs.deseq, brain_region == "Hippocampus" & log2FoldChange > 0, select="gene", drop=T)
a <- subset(st.DEGs.wilcox, select="gene", drop=T)
b <- subset(st.DEGs.mast, select="gene", drop=T)
c <- subset(st.DEGs.deseq, select="gene", drop=T)
length(intersect(a, b))
# [1] 2380
length(intersect(a, c))
# [1] 60
length(intersect(b, c))
# [1] 60
length(Reduce(intersect, list(a, b, c))) # 60
# library(VennDiagram)
# inter <- get.venn.partitions(list(a=a, b=b, c=c))
#
# sum(inter$..count..)
#
# # 1. Display Venn diagrams of DEGs identified in 12 brain regions separately.
# # 2. Display the percentage of each combination of elements relative to the total elements for DEGs identified in 12 brain regions.
# # 3. Display the Venn diagram of all DEGs.
# # 4. Display the Upset plot of all DEGs.
#
# png("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/testmin.png", width=2000, height=1800)
# par(mfrow=c(4,3))
# # or: grid.layout(ncol = 2,nrow = 3)
#
# for (i in unique(st.DEGs.wilcox$brain_region)) {
# a <- subset(st.DEGs.wilcox, brain_region == i & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", select="gene", drop=T)
# b <- subset(st.DEGs.mast, brain_region == i & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", select="gene", drop=T)
# c <- subset(st.DEGs.deseq, brain_region == i, select="gene", drop=T)
# p <- venn.diagram(x = list(wilcox=a, MAST=b, DEseq2=c), main = i, filename=NULL, output=T)
# grid.draw(p)
# }
#
# dev.off()
# # Failed, all images overlapped.
library(ggvenn)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
p.list <- list()
n <- 1
for (i in unique(st.DEGs.wilcox$brain_region)) {
  a <- subset(st.DEGs.wilcox, brain_region == i & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", select="gene", drop=T)
  b <- subset(st.DEGs.mast, brain_region == i & comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", select="gene", drop=T)
  c <- subset(st.DEGs.deseq, brain_region == i, select="gene", drop=T)
  p <- ggvenn(list(wilcox=a, MAST=b, DEseq2=c))
  p <- p + labs(title=i) +
    theme(text = element_text(family="Arial"),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size=16))
  p.list[[n]] <- p
  n <- n + 1
}
pall <- wrap_plots(p.list, ncol = 4)
ggsave("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/testmin.jpg", pall, width=20, height=15)
# 1. Label each small plot with the corresponding brain region in the title.
# 2. Enlarge the image so that the middle part is visible.
# 3. Use Arial font.
```

**Statistics of DEGs Identification Results:**
```{r nDEGsStatistics, eval=FALSE}
# Statistics of DEGs identification results.
# You can plot a line graph of the total number of DEGs identified under different log2fc thresholds to determine if there is an "inflection point".
# Is there a "gold standard" for single-cell log2fc? Answer: No.
nDEGs.vector <- c()
n <- 1
for (log2fc in seq(0, 1, 0.05)){
  file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".csv")
  DEGs.df <- read.csv(file.name, row.names=1)
  total.DEGs <- length(unique(unlist(strsplit(DEGs.df$geneList, ";"))))
  nDEGs.vector[n] <- total.DEGs
  n <- n + 1
}
png("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.nDEGs_tilde_log2fc.png")
plot(seq(0, 1, 0.05), nDEGs.vector, xlab = "log2fc", ylab="number of DEGs")
dev.off()
# It can be seen that the inflection point is around 0.3. If you want to set three thresholds (high, medium, low), it is more appropriate to set them at 0.2, 0.3, and 0.4.
# Default threshold of Seurat::FindMarkers: log2fc = 0.25; min.pct = 0.1
for (log2fc in c(0.2, 0.3, 0.4)){
  file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".csv")
  DEGs.df <- read.csv(file.name, row.names=1)
  # Compare the number of genes identified across different brain regions and comparison combinations
  p <- ggplot(DEGs.df, aes(direction, numOfGene, fill=direction)) +
    geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge") +
    facet_grid(comparison~cellType) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(up="red", down="blue")) +
    theme(title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
          strip.text = element_text(size=10, face="bold"),
          axis.text.x = element_blank())
  out.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                     log2fc, ".nDEGs.png")
  ggsave(out.name, p, width=14, height=10)
}
# Describe which factors nDEGs is related to. nDEGs can to some extent represent which brain region is more affected.
# However, it is not necessarily the case. nDEGs may also be related to the number of spots in the brain region.
log2fc <- 0.3
file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".csv")
DEGs.df <- read.csv(file.name, row.names=1)
# The smaller the AIC, the better the model
m <- glm(numOfGene~cellType + comparison + direction +
           cellType:comparison +
           cellType:direction +
           direction:comparison,
         data=DEGs.df)
summary(m) # AIC=531.08
m1 <- glm(numOfGene~cellType + comparison + direction +
           cellType:comparison +
           cellType:direction +
           direction:comparison,
         data=DEGs.df, family = poisson())
summary(m1) # AIC=474.27
m1.5 <- glm(numOfGene~cellType + comparison + direction +
           cellType:comparison +
           cellType:direction +
           direction:comparison + cellType:comparison:direction,
         data=DEGs.df, family = poisson())
summary(m1.5) # AIC: 424.44
m2 <- glm(numOfGene~comparison + direction, data=DEGs.df, family=poisson())
summary(m2)# AIC=1080.6
m3 <- glm(numOfGene~comparison + direction + comparison:direction, data=DEGs.df, family=poisson())
summary(m3)# AIC: 901.71
m4 <- glm(numOfGene~cellType, data=DEGs.df, family=poisson()) #
summary(m4) # AIC: 779.25
m5 <- glm(numOfGene~cellType+comparison, data=DEGs.df, family=poisson()) #
summary(m5) # AIC: 765.83
m6 <- glm(numOfGene~cellType+comparison+cellType:comparison, data=DEGs.df, family=poisson()) #
summary(m6) # AIC: 720.87
# A total of 371 DEGs were identified that showed differential expression between treatments in at least one brain region.
length(unique(unlist(strsplit(DEGs.df$geneList, ";"))))
# The average number of DEGs identified per brain region for AD_vs_WT, PL_vs_AD, and PL_vs_WT is...
aggregate(numOfGene~comparison, FUN=mean, data=DEGs.df)
# The brain regions with the highest number of DEGs for AD_vs_WT, PL_vs_AD, and PL_vs_WT are:...
DEGs.agg <- aggregate(numOfGene~cellType+comparison, FUN=mean, data=DEGs.df)
DEGs.agg[order(DEGs.agg$comparison, DEGs.agg$numOfGene), ]
```

**Similarity Analysis of DEGs Between Brain Regions:**
Identify brain region-specific DEGs and shared DEGs, and describe them. Some brain regions have fewer DEGs. When calculating shared DEGs between brain regions, only the top three brain regions with the highest number of DEGs can be considered. To avoid the situation where some brain regions are too similar, making it difficult to identify brain region-specific DEGs, a clustering analysis can be performed between brain regions.

```{r brainRegionSimilarity, eval=FALSE}
#-----------------------#
# Similarity of DEGs between brain regions. #
#-----------------------#
jaccard <- function(a, b) {
    intersection = length(intersect(a, b))
    union = length(a) + length(b) - intersection
    return (intersection/union)
}
log2fc <- 0.3
file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".csv")
DEGs.df <- read.csv(file.name, row.names=1)
# Merge up and down regulation. Separate different contrasts.
for (Contrast in unique(DEGs.df$comparison)){
  # Contrast <- "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"
  DEGs.df.sub <- DEGs.df[DEGs.df$comparison==Contrast, ]
  DEGs.df.sub <- aggregate(geneList~cellType, FUN=function(x){
    x <- x[x!=""]
    x <- paste(x, collapse=";")
    x <- paste(unique(strsplit(x, ";")[[1]]), collapse = ";")
  }, data=DEGs.df.sub)
  row.names(DEGs.df.sub) <- DEGs.df.sub$cellType
 
  brain.regions <- unique(DEGs.df$cellType)
  similarity.df <- data.frame(x=rep(brain.regions, each=length(brain.regions)),
                              y=rep(brain.regions, length(brain.regions)))
  similarity.df <- similarity.df[similarity.df$x!=similarity.df$y, ]
 
  similarity.df$jaccard <- sapply(1:nrow(similarity.df), function(i){
    a <- similarity.df[i, "x"]
    b <- similarity.df[i, "y"]
    a.genes <- strsplit(DEGs.df.sub[a, "geneList"], ";")[[1]]
    b.genes <- strsplit(DEGs.df.sub[b, "geneList"], ";")[[1]]
    jaccard(a.genes, b.genes)
  })
  library(reshape2)
  similarity.df <- dcast(similarity.df, x ~ y)
  similarity.df <- data.frame(similarity.df[, -1], row.names=similarity.df$x)
  library(pheatmap)
  png(paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/brain_region_similarity.", Contrast, ".png"), width=700, height=700)
  pheatmap(similarity.df, fontsize=14)
  dev.off()
}
# Module-specific / shared DEGs
DEGs.df.sub <- DEGs.df.sub <- DEGs.df[DEGs.df$comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", ]
DEGs.df.sub <- aggregate(geneList~cellType, FUN=function(x){
  x <- x[x!=""]
  paste(x, collapse=";")
}, data=DEGs.df.sub)
row.names(DEGs.df.sub) <- DEGs.df.sub$cellType
brain.region_to_module <- c(rep("module1", 6), rep("module2", 2), rep("module3", 2), "module4")
names(brain.region_to_module) <- c("Isocortex", "Third Ventricle", "Cortical subplate",
                            "Striatum", "Hippocampus", "Meninges", "Fiber tracts", "Thalamus",
                            "Hypothalamus", "Olfactory area", "Lateral Ventricle")
DEGs.df.sub$module <- brain.region_to_module[DEGs.df.sub$cellType]
DEGs.df.sub.agg <- aggregate(geneList~module, data=DEGs.df.sub, FUN=function(x){
  x <- x[x!=""]
  x <- paste(x, collapse=";")
  x <- paste(unique(strsplit(x, ";")[[1]]), collapse = ";")
})
m1.gl <- subset(DEGs.df.sub.agg, module=="module1", select="geneList", drop=T)
m1.gl <- strsplit(m1.gl, ";")[[1]]
m2.gl <- subset(DEGs.df.sub.agg, module=="module2", select="geneList", drop=T)
m2.gl <- strsplit(m2.gl, ";")[[1]]
m3.gl <- subset(DEGs.df.sub.agg, module=="module3", select="geneList", drop=T)
m3.gl <- strsplit(m3.gl, ";")[[1]]
module.genes.list <- list(m1=m1.gl, m2=m2.gl, m3=m3.gl)
out.list <- list(
  module.inter = Reduce(intersect, module.genes.list),
  m1.specific = setdiff(setdiff(m1.gl, m2.gl), m3.gl),
  m2.specific = setdiff(setdiff(m2.gl, m1.gl), m3.gl),
  m3.specific = setdiff(setdiff(m3.gl, m1.gl), m2.gl)
)
out.list <- sapply(out.list, paste, collapse=",")
out.df <- data.frame(V1=names(out.list), V2=out.list)
write.table(out.df, file = "001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/module_specific_inter.txt",
            row.names = F, col.names = F, sep = "\t", quote = F)
system("../script/metascape 001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/module_specific_inter.txt 001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/")
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/003/st.DEGs.csv](data/st/003/st.DEGs.csv): DEGs table filtered only by padj < 0.05. One gene per row, including all brain regions (excluding C12).
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.nDEGs_tilde_log2fc.png](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.nDEGs_tilde_log2fc.png){#wilcox-each-cell-nDEGs-tilde-log2fc-png target="_blank"}: Scatter plot of nDEG ~ log2fc, providing a basis for setting the log2fc threshold.
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.csv](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.csv){#wilcox-each_cell-log2fc-0-2-csv}: DEGs list identified using `Seurat::FindMarkers`.
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.csv](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.csv){#wilcox-each-cell-log2fc-0-3-csv}
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.4.csv](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.4.csv)
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.nDEGs.png](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.2.nDEGs.png){target="_blank"}: Bar plot showing the number of up/down-regulated DEGs in different brain regions and comparison combinations.
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.nDEGs.png](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.nDEGs.png){#wilcox-each-cell-log2fc-0-3-nDEGs-png target="_blank"}
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.4.nDEGs.png](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.4.nDEGs.png){target="_blank"}
- [data/st/003/module_specific_inter.txt](data/st/003/module_specific_inter.txt){#module-specific-inter-txt target="_blank"}: Shared and specific DEGs for each module.

```{r brainRegionSimilarity1, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/st/003/brain_region_similarity.FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl.png")
```
```{r brainRegionSimilarity2, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/st/003/brain_region_similarity.PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl.png")
```
```{r moduleSpecificInterMetascape, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Functional enrichment analysis of shared and specific DEGs across modules."}
knitr::include_graphics("data/st/003/module_specific_inter_metascape.png")
```

**Result Description**

**Selection of log2fc threshold.** According to the scatter plot of nDEG ~ log2fc, there is an inflection point around log2fc = 0.3 ([wilcox.each_cell.nDEGs_tilde_log2fc.png](#wilcox-each-cell-nDEGs-tilde-log2fc-png)). Therefore, 0.3 was chosen as the threshold for subsequent analysis.

**Differences in the number of DEGs across brain regions.** Using the wilcox method, with thresholds of log2fc=0.3, min.pct=0.1, and FDR<0.05, a total of 371 DEGs were identified that showed differential expression between treatments in at least one brain region. Drug treatment (PL_vs_AD) mainly caused downregulation. The brain regions with the most downregulated DEGs after drug treatment (PL_vs_AD) were Olfactory area, Fiber tracts, and Thalamus, while the brain regions with the most upregulated DEGs were Olfactory area, Cortical subplate, and Hypothalamus ([wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.csv](#wilcox-each-cell-log2fc-0-3-csv); [wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.nDEGs.png](#wilcox-each-cell-log2fc-0-3-nDEGs-png)).

**Similarity of DEGs between brain regions.**  
For AD_vs_WT, the DEGs similarity was high among Hippocampus, Isocortex, Thalamus, Fiber tracts, and Olfactory area (Figure \@ref(fig:brainRegionSimilarity1)). What pathways are the shared DEGs in these brain regions enriched in?  
For PL_vs_AD, Isocortex, Third ventricle, Cortical subplate, Striatum, Hippocampus, and Meninges (module1) showed high DEGs similarity with each other. In addition, Fiber tracts and Thalamus (module2) also showed high similarity (Figure \@ref(fig:brainRegionSimilarity2)). The remaining Hypothalamus and Olfactory area were classified as module3. Describe the module-specific DEGs. The shared and specific DEGs of these 3 modules were identified and subjected to Metascape functional enrichment analysis. The results showed that the shared DEGs of the three modules were mainly enriched in pathways represented by "synapse pruning" and "microglia cell activation". Under the threshold of qvalue < 0.05 (log(qvalue) < -1.30103), neither module1 nor module2 specific DEGs were enriched in any pathways. Module3 was enriched in pathways represented by "synaptic vesicle cycle" ([module_specific_inter.txt](#module-specific-inter-txt); Figure \@ref(fig:moduleSpecificInterMetascape)).

### Rescued genes: lfc 0.3
- Identify rescued genes and describe them. Number of DEGs in different brain regions?
- The rescued DEGs table can undergo the same downstream analyses as the original DEGs table: functional enrichment analysis, investigation of genes of interest.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Original script: script/003identify_DEGs.filter_DEGs.r
source("script/utils/filter_DEGs.r") # Load the get_rescued method.
# get rescued genes.
log2fc <- 0.3
file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".csv")
DEGs.df <- read.csv(file.name, row.names=1)
DEGs.rescued <- get_rescued(DEGs.df)
file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".rescued.csv")
write.csv(DEGs.rescued, file.name)
p <- ggplot(DEGs.rescued, aes(direction, numOfGene, fill=direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge") +
  facet_grid(.~cellType) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(up="red", down="blue")) +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(size=10, face="bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_blank())
out.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                   log2fc, ".rescued.nDEGs.png")
ggsave(out.name, p, width=14, height=3)
```

### Rescued genes: lfc 0
```{r}
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Do not set log2fc threshold
# source("script/utils/filter_DEGs.r") # This method is designed based on the one-row-multiple-genes format.
st.DEGs <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
# Method 1: Subset first, then merge.
# Merge the two comparison combination tables based on gene, brain region, and comparison, then screen for genes with opposite log2FC directions.
df1 <- subset(st.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl", select=c("gene", "brain_region", "avg_log2FC"))
df2 <- subset(st.DEGs, comparison == "FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", select=c("gene", "brain_region", "avg_log2FC"))
colnames(df1) <- sub("avg_log2FC", "log2FC.PL_vs_AD", colnames(df1))
colnames(df2) <- sub("avg_log2FC", "log2FC.AD_vs_WT", colnames(df2))
df.merged <- merge(df1, df2, by=c("gene", "brain_region"))
df.rescued <- subset(df.merged, df.merged$log2FC.PL_vs_AD * df.merged$log2FC.AD_vs_WT < 0)
df.rescued.up <- subset(df.rescued, log2FC.PL_vs_AD > 0)
writeLines(df.rescued.up$gene, "001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.up_rescued.list")
file.name <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                      log2fc, ".rescued.csv")
write.csv(DEGs.rescued, file.name)
# Method 2: Use dcast directly, which is more concise.
st.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.sub <- subset(st.DEGs, comparison != "PLX5622_vs_WTCtrl")
st.DEGs.wide <- dcast(gene+brain_region~comparison, value.var = "avg_log2FC", data=st.DEGs.sub)
st.DEGs.wide <- na.omit(st.DEGs.wide)
st.DEGs.wide <- subset(st.DEGs.wide, PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl*FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl < 0) # Genes with opposite change directions in the two comparison combinations.
st.DEGs.wide$direction <- ifelse(st.DEGs.wide$PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl > 0, "up rescued", "down rescued")
write.csv(st.DEGs.wide, "../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.rescued.csv")
length(unique(st.DEGs.wide$gene))
# [1] 305
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.rescued.csv](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.rescued.csv): Rescued DEGs table.
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.rescued.nDEGs.png](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.rescued.nDEGs.png){target="_blank"}: Comparison plot of the number of rescued DEGs across different brain regions.

#---
## Spot DEGs Classification Discussion: By Cell Type
### Gene Filtering
Term definitions:
- microglia enriched: Level 1 microglia marker genes.
- microglia specific: Genes that belong to microglia enriched genes but do not belong to enriched genes of other cell types.

#### micro enriched
Using Level 1 microglia markers
```{r}
# Load spot DEGs
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
head(spot.DEGs)
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region comparison gene
# Thy1 0.000000e+00 2.2648989 0.999 0.996 0.000000e+00 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Thy1
# Gfap 1.040859e-252 1.1939180 0.854 0.268 1.930897e-248 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Gfap
# Ctsd 8.787947e-158 0.9353749 0.996 0.994 1.630252e-153 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Ctsd
# Tyrobp 2.050541e-145 0.9585237 0.840 0.508 3.803959e-141 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Tyrobp
# Cst7 7.212608e-136 0.4824200 0.379 0.003 1.338011e-131 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Cst7
# B2m 3.464400e-131 0.6965407 0.976 0.917 6.426808e-127 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl B2m
# Load Level 1 marker genes.
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
head(cell.DEGs)
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj cluster gene
# St18 0 2.026990 0.965 0.180 0 Oligo St18
# Mbp 0 2.001661 0.992 0.387 0 Oligo Mbp
# Prr5l 0 1.964242 0.941 0.114 0 Oligo Prr5l
# Neat1 0 1.845240 0.923 0.211 0 Oligo Neat1
# Mobp 0 1.800589 0.893 0.140 0 Oligo Mobp
# Mag 0 1.624231 0.860 0.156 0 Oligo Mag
# Calculate the number of micro specific ST DEGs and fill in the section mentioned by Teacher Ming today.
nrow(spot.DEGs) == nrow(subset(spot.DEGs, p_val_adj < 0.05)) # TRUE
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
cell.DEGs.sub <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == "Micro")
length(unique(spot.DEGs.sub$gene))
length(intersect(spot.DEGs.sub$gene, cell.DEGs.sub$gene))
# 120
# We observed that 120 of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in microglia
spot.DEGs.sub.micro <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% cell.DEGs.sub$gene)
subset(spot.DEGs.sub.micro, avg_log2FC > 0)
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region comparison gene
# Gm424181 6.272133e-18 0.3068101 0.998 0.996 1.163543e-13 Hypothalamus PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Gm42418
# Apoe2 8.322432e-09 0.1322606 0.998 0.998 1.543894e-04 Hypothalamus PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Apoe
# Gm424182 3.172949e-09 0.2123767 1.000 1.000 5.886137e-05 Cortical subplate PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Gm42418
# Gm424185 1.578519e-14 0.2395111 1.000 1.000 2.928311e-10 Thalamus PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Gm42418
# Gm424186 6.455716e-60 0.7325213 1.000 1.000 1.197600e-55 Olfactory area PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Gm42418
# Apoe11 3.932276e-09 0.1616117 1.000 1.000 7.294765e-05 Olfactory area PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Apoe
# Tsc22d4 1.129232e-06 0.1407847 0.897 0.840 2.094839e-02 Olfactory area PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Tsc22d4
# Upregulated genes include: Apoe, Gm42418, Tsc22d4
down <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub.micro, spot.DEGs.sub.micro$avg_log2FC < 0)
up <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub.micro, spot.DEGs.sub.micro$avg_log2FC > 0)
intersect(down$gene, up$gene) # 0
length(unique(down$gene)) / length(unique(spot.DEGs.sub.micro$gene))
# [1] 0.975
# and 97.5% of them were downregulated
a <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub.micro, brain_region %in% c("Thalamus", "Olfactory area"))
length(unique(a$gene )) / length(unique(spot.DEGs.sub.micro$gene))
# 0.9666667
# 92.7% of the microglial-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus
# Draw heatmap of micro specific ST DEGs.
# Draw heatmap of Astro specific ST DEGs.
# Use MitoCarta3.0 instead of genes in the "GO: oxidative phosphorylation pathway" for re-analysis.
# Existing issue: Microglia markers may need to remove genes that are also highly expressed in other cell types.
```

#### micro specific
Belong to Level 1 microglia marker & do not belong to other cell markers
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
df1 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == "Micro")
df2 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster != "Micro")
micro.specific <- setdiff(df1$gene, df2$gene)
length(micro.specific) # 557
spot.DEGs.micro <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% micro.specific)
intersect(micro.specific, c("Apoe", "Gm42418", "Tsc22d4")) # character(0)
X <- length(unique(spot.DEGs.micro$gene)) # 103
down <- subset(spot.DEGs.micro, spot.DEGs.micro$avg_log2FC < 0)
Y <- length(unique(down$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.micro$gene)) # 1
a <- subset(spot.DEGs.micro, brain_region %in% c("Thalamus", "Olfactory area"))
Z <- length(unique(a$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.micro$gene)) # 0.961165
out <- paste0("We observed that ", X, " of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in microglia, and ", Y, "% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). ", round(Z, 1), "% of the microglial-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus")
print(out)
```

#### astro enriched
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
ct <- "Astro"
ct.enriched <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
spot.DEGs.ct <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.enriched$gene)
length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene)) # 61
ct.enriched2 <- subset(ct.enriched, abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.4)
spot.DEGs.ct2 <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.enriched2$gene)
length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct2$gene)) # 43
# Pathway enrichment analysis.
spot.DEGs.ct2.up <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct2, avg_log2FC > 0)$gene)
spot.DEGs.ct2.down <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct2, avg_log2FC < 0)$gene)
writeLines(c(paste0("up\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct2.up, collapse=",")),
           paste0("down\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct2.down, collapse=","))),
           "../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/Astro_enriched.txt")
```

#### astro specific
Belong to Level 1 astrocyte marker & do not belong to other cell markers
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
ct <- "Astro"
df1 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
df2 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster != ct)
ct.specific <- setdiff(df1$gene, df2$gene)
length(ct.specific) # 557
spot.DEGs.ct <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.specific)
# intersect(micro.specific, c("Apoe", "Gm42418", "Tsc22d4")) # character(0)
X <- length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene)) # 103
down <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, spot.DEGs.ct$avg_log2FC < 0)
Y <- length(unique(down$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene)) # 1
a <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, brain_region %in% c("Thalamus", "Olfactory area"))
Z <- length(unique(a$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene)) # 0.961165
out <- paste0("We observed that ", round(X, 1), " of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in ", ct, ", and ", round(Y, 1), "% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). ", round(Z, 1), "% of the ", ct, "-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus")
print(out)
# Pathway enrichment analysis.
spot.DEGs.ct.up <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct, avg_log2FC > 0)$gene)
spot.DEGs.ct.down <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct, avg_log2FC < 0)$gene)
writeLines(c(paste0("up\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct.up, collapse=",")),
           paste0("down\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct.down, collapse=","))),
           "../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/Astro_specific.txt")
```
```
"We observed that 28 of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in Astro, and 46.4% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). 82.1% of the Astro-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus"
```

#### Glu specific
Belong to Level 1 Glu marker & do not belong to other cell markers
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
ct <- "Glu"
df1 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
df2 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster != ct)
ct.specific <- setdiff(df1$gene, df2$gene)
length(ct.specific)
spot.DEGs.ct <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.specific)
X <- length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
down <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, spot.DEGs.ct$avg_log2FC < 0)
Y <- length(unique(down$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
a <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, brain_region %in% c("Thalamus", "Olfactory area"))
Z <- length(unique(a$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
out <- paste0("We observed that ", round(X, 1), " of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in ", ct, ", and ", round(Y, 1), "% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). ", round(Z, 1), "% of the ", ct, "-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus")
print(out)
# Pathway enrichment analysis.
spot.DEGs.ct.up <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct, avg_log2FC > 0)$gene)
spot.DEGs.ct.down <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct, avg_log2FC < 0)$gene)
writeLines(c(paste0("up\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct.up, collapse=",")),
           paste0("down\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct.down, collapse=","))),
           paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_specific.txt"))
```

#### Oligo enriched
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
ct <- "Oligo"
ct.enriched <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
spot.DEGs.ct <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.enriched$gene)
length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene)) # 49
App.cutoff <- abs(subset(ct.enriched, gene == "App")$avg_log2FC) # 0.3801595
ct.enriched2 <- subset(ct.enriched, abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.4)
spot.DEGs.ct2 <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.enriched2$gene)
length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct2$gene)) # 30
# Pathway enrichment analysis.
# PL vs AD up, down.
spot.DEGs.ct2.up <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct2, avg_log2FC > 0)$gene)
spot.DEGs.ct2.down <- unique(subset(spot.DEGs.ct2, avg_log2FC < 0)$gene)
writeLines(c(paste0("up\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct2.up, collapse=",")),
           paste0("down\t",paste(spot.DEGs.ct2.down, collapse=","))),
           paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_enriched.txt"))
```

#### Oligo specific
```{r}
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
spot.DEGs.sub <- subset(spot.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
ct <- "Oligo"
df1 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
df2 <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster != ct)
ct.specific <- setdiff(df1$gene, df2$gene)
length(ct.specific)
spot.DEGs.ct <- subset(spot.DEGs.sub, gene %in% ct.specific)
X <- length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
down <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, spot.DEGs.ct$avg_log2FC < 0)
Y <- length(unique(down$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
a <- subset(spot.DEGs.ct, brain_region %in% c("Thalamus", "Olfactory area"))
Z <- length(unique(a$gene))*100 / length(unique(spot.DEGs.ct$gene))
out <- paste0("We observed that ", round(X, 1), " of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in ", ct, ", and ", round(Y, 1), "% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). ", round(Z, 1), "% of the ", ct, "-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus")
print(out)
```
```
"We observed that 33 of PLX5622-responsive genes were enriched in Oligo, and 84.8% of them were downregulated (Fig. Ext. 4e). 81.8% of the Oligo-specific PLX5622-responsive genes were located in the two brain regions with the highest Aβ burden—the olfactory cortical area and thalamus"
```

### DEGs Heatmap Drawing
#### Load Data
```{r}
# Load spot DEGs
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
head(spot.DEGs)
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region comparison gene
# Thy1 0.000000e+00 2.2648989 0.999 0.996 0.000000e+00 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Thy1
# Gfap 1.040859e-252 1.1939180 0.854 0.268 1.930897e-248 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Gfap
# Ctsd 8.787947e-158 0.9353749 0.996 0.994 1.630252e-153 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Ctsd
# Tyrobp 2.050541e-145 0.9585237 0.840 0.508 3.803959e-141 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Tyrobp
# Cst7 7.212608e-136 0.4824200 0.379 0.003 1.338011e-131 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Cst7
# B2m 3.464400e-131 0.6965407 0.976 0.917 6.426808e-127 Isocortex FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl B2m
# Load Level 1 marker genes.
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
head(cell.DEGs)
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj cluster gene
# St18 0 2.026990 0.965 0.180 0 Oligo St18
# Mbp 0 2.001661 0.992 0.387 0 Oligo Mbp
# Prr5l 0 1.964242 0.941 0.114 0 Oligo Prr5l
# Neat1 0 1.845240 0.923 0.211 0 Oligo Neat1
# Mobp 0 1.800589 0.893 0.140 0 Oligo Mobp
# Mag 0 1.624231 0.860 0.156 0 Oligo Mag
```

#### Style 3: Detailed Adjustments (Astro, Oligo)
Code modifications:
todo:
- Add gaps for different clusters? Is there any description of clusters in the paper?
- Use color to indicate brain regions?

Completed:
- Adjusted image size.
- Gene names in italics.
- Added rescued column.
- Olfactory cortical area.
- Whether to change the color of comparison combinations? Remove it directly, as it seems to have little effect.
- Removed anno.col and anno.row.
- Unified heatmap colors?
- Font size unified to 8.
- Font unified to Arial.
- Cluster IDs numbered sequentially from top to bottom. Make the source data consistent with the clusters shown in the figure.

Adobe Illustrator modifications:
todo:
a. Label the two comparison combinations above the image.
b. Add scaled lfc legend title.
c. Place the Rescued genes color legend below the scaled lfc legend.
d. Label directly above.
e. Cluster IDs numbered sequentially from top to bottom.

#### Astro, Oligo (Enriched)
```{r st-degs-heatmap-pdf}
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
# --- 0. Read data and define order (unchanged) ---
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
br.order <- rev(Abeta.merged$brain_region)
br.order <- br.order[!grepl("Ventricle", br.order)]
# --- 1. Prepare data ---
# Assume cell.DEGs and spot.DEGs already exist in the environment
for (ct in c("Astro", "Oligo")) {
  # ct <- "Astro"
  only_PL_vs_AD <- TRUE
 
  # ct_genes <- setdiff(cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster == ct],
  # cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster != ct])
 
  # ct.enriched <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster == ct)
  ct.enriched2 <- subset(cell.DEGs, abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.4 & cluster == ct)
 
  sub_dat <- subset(spot.DEGs, gene %in% ct.enriched2$gene &
                    comparison %in% c("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"))
 
  # [Modification 1]: Ensure the brain region list is sorted according to br.order, not alphabetical order
  # This step is very important. First take the intersection to keep only existing brain regions while maintaining the order of br.order
  available_regions <- unique(sub_dat$brain_region)
  sorted_regions <- br.order[br.order %in% available_regions]
 
  if (only_PL_vs_AD) {
    all_genes <- unique(subset(sub_dat, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")$gene)
  } else {
    all_genes <- unique(sub_dat$gene)
  }
 
 
  # --- 2. Matrix extraction function (fine-tuned to adapt to sorting) ---
  get_matrix <- function(comp_name) {
    tmp <- dcast(subset(sub_dat, comparison == comp_name),
                 gene ~ brain_region, value.var = "avg_log2FC", fill = 0)
    rownames(tmp) <- tmp$gene
    tmp <- tmp[, -1]
   
    # Initialize matrix
    res <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_genes), ncol = length(sorted_regions),
                  dimnames = list(all_genes, sorted_regions)) # Note: sorted_regions is used here
   
    common_g <- intersect(rownames(tmp), all_genes)
    common_r <- intersect(colnames(tmp), sorted_regions)
   
    if(length(common_g) > 0 && length(common_r) > 0){
      res[common_g, common_r] <- as.matrix(tmp[common_g, common_r])
    }
   
    # [Core of Modification 1]: Force reordering according to sorted_regions
    res <- res[, sorted_regions, drop=FALSE]
    return(res)
  }
 
  mat_AD <- get_matrix("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl")
  mat_PL <- get_matrix("PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
 
  # --- 3. Merge and process column names ---
 
  mat_final <- cbind(mat_AD, mat_PL)
 
  # Force modification of column names to make them unique
  colnames(mat_final) <- c(paste0("AD_", sorted_regions), paste0("PL_", sorted_regions))
  sorted_regions.renamed <- sub("Olfactory area", "Olfactory cortical area", sorted_regions)
 
  # --- 4. Scale (vectorized method) ---
  row_max <- apply(abs(mat_final), 1, max)
  # Prevent division by zero
  row_max[row_max == 0] <- 1
  mat_scaled <- mat_final / row_max
 
  # --- 5. Pre-compute clustering to generate Cluster annotation (Modification 2 & 3) ---
 
  # To obtain specific Cluster numbers (1-6), we need to perform hierarchical clustering before plotting
  # Calculate distance and clustering tree
  gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled, method = "euclidean")
  gene_hclust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "complete")
 
  # Cut genes into 6 clusters
  gene_clusters <- cutree(gene_hclust, k = 6)
 
  # Sort the matrix according to clustering results
  mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_hclust$order, ]
 
  # Build row annotation data frame (Row Annotation)
  # This will add a color bar on the left side of the heatmap with a legend indicating which Cluster
  # anno_row <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(paste0("Cluster ", gene_clusters)))
  # rownames(anno_row) <- rownames(mat_scaled)
 
  # rescued genes annotation
  st.DEGs.rescued <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.rescued.csv", row.names=1)
 
  # In R, I have a dataframe with two columns: gene and direction, both strings. Now I need to group by gene and merge the directions belonging to the same gene into one string separated by commas. Please help me write the code.
  # Using dplyr as an example
  library(dplyr)
  result <- st.DEGs.rescued %>%
    group_by(gene) %>%
    summarise(direction = paste(unique(direction), collapse = ","))
 
  # Remove contradictions between brain regions.
  result2 <- subset(result, direction=="down rescued" | direction=="up rescued")
  result2 <- data.frame(result2)
  row.names(result2) <- result2$gene
 
  anno_row <- data.frame(Rescued=result2[rownames(mat_scaled), "direction"], row.names=rownames(mat_scaled))
  anno_row$Rescued[is.na(anno_row$Rescued)] <- "None"
 
  # --- 6. Plot preparation ---
 
  # Column annotation (Column Annotation)
  # anno_col <- data.frame(Group = factor(rep(c("AD vs WT", "PL vs AD"), each = length(sorted_regions)),
  # levels = c("AD vs WT", "PL vs AD")))
  # rownames(anno_col) <- colnames(mat_scaled)
 
  # Color settings
  # Automatically assign colors to 6 Clusters, or you can specify manually
  # cluster_colors <- brewer.pal(6, "Set2")
  # names(cluster_colors) <- paste0("Cluster ", 1:6)
 
  # anno_colors <- list(
  # Group = c("AD vs WT"="#E69F00", "PL vs AD"="#56B4E9"),
  # Cluster = cluster_colors # Add color mapping for Cluster
  # )
 
  anno_colors <- list(Rescued=c("None"="grey", "up rescued"="red", "down rescued"="blue"))
 
  # Main panel color settings
  my_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(100)
 
  # --- 7. Final plotting ---
  p <- pheatmap(mat_scaled,
           # Row clustering settings
           # cluster_rows = gene_hclust, # Use the pre-computed clustering tree
           cluster_rows = F,
           # cutree_rows = 6, # [Modification 2]: Divide rows into 6 clusters and add gaps
          
           # Column clustering settings
           cluster_cols = FALSE, # [Modification 1]: Turn off column clustering and strictly follow the sorted_regions we set
          
           # Colors and breaks
           color = my_colors, # How to control the length and width of pheatmap legend?
           breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
          
           # Annotations
           # annotation_col = anno_col,
           annotation_row = anno_row, # How to display pheatmap anno row but not show the corresponding legend?
           annotation_legend = FALSE,
           annotation_colors = anno_colors,
          
           # Gap settings
           gaps_col = length(sorted_regions), # Add gap between the two comparison groups
          
           # Label settings
           labels_col = rep(sorted_regions.renamed, 2), # Use original brain region names as labels
           show_rownames = T, # Usually do not show row names when there are too many genes; change to TRUE if needed
          
           main = paste0("Transcriptional alterations of ", ct, " \nenriched genes across comparisons"), # I have a heatmap showing scaled log2 fold change of genes highly expressed in astrocytes across two comparison combinations. What should the title of this heatmap be?
           # border_color = NA, # Having borders looks better
           # fontsize=7, # Title font is set below, not bold, to save space.
           fontsize_row=8,
           fontsize_col=8,
           silent = T
  )
 
  # Modify font to italic.
  library(grid)
  idx <- grep("row_names", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontface = "italic", fontsize=8)
 
  idx <- grep("main", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontsize=8)
 
  if (ct == "Astro") {
    width=3.68
    height=6
  } else if (ct == "Oligo") {
    width=3.68
    height=5.2
  }
 
  # Draw pdf
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.pdf")
  cairo_pdf(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial")
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
 
  # When using R's png function to plot and specifying Arial font, it shows: Windows font database does not have such a font family. How to solve?
  # Answer: Add the following line.
  windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
  png(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial", units = "in", res=300)
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}
```
output:
```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/Oligo_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
knitr::include_graphics("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/Astro_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
```


#### Astro (specific)
```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
# --- 0. Read data and define order (unchanged) ---
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
br.order <- rev(Abeta.merged$brain_region)
br.order <- br.order[!grepl("Ventricle", br.order)]
ST_DEGs_heatmap <- function(glist, p.title, width=3.68, height=6) {
 
  only_PL_vs_AD <- TRUE
 
  sub_dat <- subset(spot.DEGs, gene %in% glist &
                    comparison %in% c("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"))
 
  # [Modification 1]: Ensure the brain region list is sorted according to br.order rather than alphabetical order
  # This step is critical. Take the intersection first to retain only existing brain regions while preserving the order of br.order
  available_regions <- unique(sub_dat$brain_region)
  sorted_regions <- br.order[br.order %in% available_regions]
 
  if (only_PL_vs_AD) {
    all_genes <- unique(subset(sub_dat, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")$gene)
  } else {
    all_genes <- unique(sub_dat$gene)
  }
 
 
  # --- 2. Matrix extraction function (fine-tuned to adapt to sorting) ---
  get_matrix <- function(comp_name) {
    tmp <- dcast(subset(sub_dat, comparison == comp_name),
                 gene ~ brain_region, value.var = "avg_log2FC", fill = 0)
    rownames(tmp) <- tmp$gene
    tmp <- tmp[, -1]
   
    # Initialize matrix
    res <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_genes), ncol = length(sorted_regions),
                  dimnames = list(all_genes, sorted_regions)) # Note: sorted_regions is used here
   
    common_g <- intersect(rownames(tmp), all_genes)
    common_r <- intersect(colnames(tmp), sorted_regions)
   
    if(length(common_g) > 0 && length(common_r) > 0){
      res[common_g, common_r] <- as.matrix(tmp[common_g, common_r])
    }
   
    # [Core of Modification 1]: Force reordering according to sorted_regions
    res <- res[, sorted_regions, drop=FALSE]
    return(res)
  }
 
  mat_AD <- get_matrix("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl")
  mat_PL <- get_matrix("PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
 
  # --- 3. Merge and process column names ---
 
  mat_final <- cbind(mat_AD, mat_PL)
 
  # Force modification of column names to make them unique
  colnames(mat_final) <- c(paste0("AD_", sorted_regions), paste0("PL_", sorted_regions))
  sorted_regions.renamed <- sub("Olfactory area", "Olfactory cortical area", sorted_regions)
 
  # --- 4. Scale (vectorized method) ---
  row_max <- apply(abs(mat_final), 1, max)
  # Prevent division by zero
  row_max[row_max == 0] <- 1
  mat_scaled <- mat_final / row_max
 
  # --- 5. Pre-compute clustering to generate Cluster annotation (Modification 2 & 3) ---
 
  # To obtain specific Cluster numbers (1-6), we need to perform hierarchical clustering before plotting
  # Calculate distance and clustering tree
  gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled, method = "euclidean")
  gene_hclust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "complete")
 
  # Cut genes into 6 clusters
  # gene_clusters <- cutree(gene_hclust, k = 6)
 
  # Sort the matrix according to clustering results
  mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_hclust$order, ]
 
  # Build row annotation data frame (Row Annotation)
  # This will add a color bar on the left side of the heatmap with a legend indicating which Cluster
  # anno_row <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(paste0("Cluster ", gene_clusters)))
  # rownames(anno_row) <- rownames(mat_scaled)
 
  # rescued genes annotation
  st.DEGs.rescued <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.rescued.csv", row.names=1)
 
  # In R, I have a dataframe with two columns: gene and direction, both strings. Now I need to group by gene and merge the directions belonging to the same gene into one string separated by commas. Please help me write the code.
  # Using dplyr as an example
  library(dplyr)
  result <- st.DEGs.rescued %>%
    group_by(gene) %>%
    summarise(direction = paste(unique(direction), collapse = ","))
 
  # Remove contradictions between brain regions.
  result2 <- subset(result, direction=="down rescued" | direction=="up rescued")
  result2 <- data.frame(result2)
  row.names(result2) <- result2$gene
 
  anno_row <- data.frame(Rescued=result2[rownames(mat_scaled), "direction"], row.names=rownames(mat_scaled))
  anno_row$Rescued[is.na(anno_row$Rescued)] <- "None"
 
  # --- 6. Plot preparation ---
 
  anno_colors <- list(Rescued=c("None"="grey", "up rescued"="red", "down rescued"="blue"))
 
  # Main panel color settings
  my_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(100)
 
  # --- 7. Final plotting ---
  p <- pheatmap(mat_scaled,
           # Row clustering settings
           # cluster_rows = gene_hclust, # Use the pre-computed clustering tree
           cluster_rows = F,
           # cutree_rows = 6, # [Modification 2]: Divide rows into 6 clusters and add gaps
          
           # Column clustering settings
           cluster_cols = FALSE, # [Modification 1]: Turn off column clustering and strictly follow the sorted_regions we set
          
           # Colors and breaks
           color = my_colors, # How to control the length and width of pheatmap legend?
           breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
          
           # Annotations
           # annotation_col = anno_col,
           annotation_row = anno_row, # How to display pheatmap anno row but not show the corresponding legend?
           annotation_legend = FALSE,
           annotation_colors = anno_colors,
          
           # Gap settings
           gaps_col = length(sorted_regions), # Add gap between the two comparison groups
          
           # Label settings
           labels_col = rep(sorted_regions.renamed, 2), # Use original brain region names as labels
           show_rownames = T, # Usually do not show row names when there are too many genes; change to TRUE if needed
          
           main = paste0("Transcriptional alterations of\n", prefix, " genes across comparisons"), # I have a heatmap showing scaled log2 fold change of genes highly expressed in astrocytes across two comparison combinations. What should the title of this heatmap be?
           # border_color = NA, # Having borders looks better
           # fontsize=7, # Title font is set below, not bold, to save space.
           fontsize_row=8,
           fontsize_col=8,
           silent = T
  )
 
  # Modify font to italic.
  library(grid)
  idx <- grep("row_names", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontface = "italic", fontsize=8)
 
  idx <- grep("main", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontsize=8)
 
  width=width
  height=height
 
  # Draw pdf
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", make.names(prefix), "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.pdf")
  cairo_pdf(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial")
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
 
  # When using R's png function to plot and specifying Arial font, it shows: Windows font database does not have such a font family. How to solve?
  # Answer: Add the following line.
  windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", make.names(prefix), "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
  png(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial", units = "in", res=300)
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}
ct <- "Astro"
ct_genes <- setdiff(cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster == ct],
                    cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster != ct])
prefix <- "Astro signature"
ST_DEGs_heatmap(ct_genes, prefix, width=3.68, height=5)
```

#### Reactive astrocytes
```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
# --- 0. Read data and define order (unchanged) ---
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
br.order <- rev(Abeta.merged$brain_region)
br.order <- br.order[!grepl("Ventricle", br.order)]
# --- 1. Prepare data ---
# Assume cell.DEGs and spot.DEGs already exist in the environment
ST_DEGs_heatmap <- function(glist, p.title, width=3.68, height=6) {
 
  only_PL_vs_AD <- TRUE
 
  sub_dat <- subset(spot.DEGs, gene %in% glist &
                    comparison %in% c("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"))
 
  # [Modification 1]: Ensure the brain region list is sorted according to br.order rather than alphabetical order
  # This step is critical. Take the intersection first to retain only existing brain regions while preserving the order of br.order
  available_regions <- unique(sub_dat$brain_region)
  sorted_regions <- br.order[br.order %in% available_regions]
 
  if (only_PL_vs_AD) {
    all_genes <- unique(subset(sub_dat, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")$gene)
  } else {
    all_genes <- unique(sub_dat$gene)
  }
 
 
  # --- 2. Matrix extraction function (fine-tuned to adapt to sorting) ---
  get_matrix <- function(comp_name) {
    tmp <- dcast(subset(sub_dat, comparison == comp_name),
                 gene ~ brain_region, value.var = "avg_log2FC", fill = 0)
    rownames(tmp) <- tmp$gene
    tmp <- tmp[, -1]
   
    # Initialize matrix
    res <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_genes), ncol = length(sorted_regions),
                  dimnames = list(all_genes, sorted_regions)) # Note: sorted_regions is used here
   
    common_g <- intersect(rownames(tmp), all_genes)
    common_r <- intersect(colnames(tmp), sorted_regions)
   
    if(length(common_g) > 0 && length(common_r) > 0){
      res[common_g, common_r] <- as.matrix(tmp[common_g, common_r])
    }
   
    # [Core of Modification 1]: Force reordering according to sorted_regions
    res <- res[, sorted_regions, drop=FALSE]
    return(res)
  }
 
  mat_AD <- get_matrix("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl")
  mat_PL <- get_matrix("PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
 
  # --- 3. Merge and process column names ---
 
  mat_final <- cbind(mat_AD, mat_PL)
 
  # Force modification of column names to make them unique
  colnames(mat_final) <- c(paste0("AD_", sorted_regions), paste0("PL_", sorted_regions))
  sorted_regions.renamed <- sub("Olfactory area", "Olfactory cortical area", sorted_regions)
 
  # --- 4. Scale (vectorized method) ---
  row_max <- apply(abs(mat_final), 1, max)
  # Prevent division by zero
  row_max[row_max == 0] <- 1
  mat_scaled <- mat_final / row_max
 
  # --- 5. Pre-compute clustering to generate Cluster annotation (Modification 2 & 3) ---
 
  # To obtain specific Cluster numbers (1-6), we need to perform hierarchical clustering before plotting
  # Calculate distance and clustering tree
  gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled, method = "euclidean")
  gene_hclust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "complete")
 
  # Cut genes into 6 clusters
  # gene_clusters <- cutree(gene_hclust, k = 6)
 
  # Sort the matrix according to clustering results
  mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_hclust$order, ]
 
  # Build row annotation data frame (Row Annotation)
  # This will add a color bar on the left side of the heatmap with a legend indicating which Cluster
  # anno_row <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(paste0("Cluster ", gene_clusters)))
  # rownames(anno_row) <- rownames(mat_scaled)
 
  # rescued genes annotation
  st.DEGs.rescued <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.rescued.csv", row.names=1)
 
  # In R, I have a dataframe with two columns: gene and direction, both strings. Now I need to group by gene and merge the directions belonging to the same gene into one string separated by commas. Please help me write the code.
  # Using dplyr as an example
  library(dplyr)
  result <- st.DEGs.rescued %>%
    group_by(gene) %>%
    summarise(direction = paste(unique(direction), collapse = ","))
 
  # Remove contradictions between brain regions.
  result2 <- subset(result, direction=="down rescued" | direction=="up rescued")
  result2 <- data.frame(result2)
  row.names(result2) <- result2$gene
 
  anno_row <- data.frame(Rescued=result2[rownames(mat_scaled), "direction"], row.names=rownames(mat_scaled))
  anno_row$Rescued[is.na(anno_row$Rescued)] <- "None"
 
  # --- 6. Plot preparation ---
 
  anno_colors <- list(Rescued=c("None"="grey", "up rescued"="red", "down rescued"="blue"))
 
  # Main panel color settings
  my_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(100)
 
  # --- 7. Final plotting ---
  p <- pheatmap(mat_scaled,
           # Row clustering settings
           # cluster_rows = gene_hclust, # Use the pre-computed clustering tree
           cluster_rows = F,
           # cutree_rows = 6, # [Modification 2]: Divide rows into 6 clusters and add gaps
          
           # Column clustering settings
           cluster_cols = FALSE, # [Modification 1]: Turn off column clustering and strictly follow the sorted_regions we set
          
           # Colors and breaks
           color = my_colors, # How to control the length and width of pheatmap legend?
           breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
          
           # Annotations
           # annotation_col = anno_col,
           annotation_row = anno_row, # How to display pheatmap anno row but not show the corresponding legend?
           annotation_legend = FALSE,
           annotation_colors = anno_colors,
          
           # Gap settings
           gaps_col = length(sorted_regions), # Add gap between the two comparison groups
          
           # Label settings
           labels_col = rep(sorted_regions.renamed, 2), # Use original brain region names as labels
           show_rownames = T, # Usually do not show row names when there are too many genes; change to TRUE if needed
          
           main = paste0("Transcriptional alterations of\n", prefix, " ST DEGs across comparisons"), # I have a heatmap showing scaled log2 fold change of genes highly expressed in astrocytes across two comparison combinations. What should the title of this heatmap be?
           # border_color = NA, # Having borders looks better
           # fontsize=7, # Title font is set below, not bold, to save space.
           fontsize_row=8,
           fontsize_col=8,
           silent = T
  )
 
  # Modify font to italic.
  library(grid)
  idx <- grep("row_names", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontface = "italic", fontsize=8)
 
  idx <- grep("main", p$gtable$layout$name)
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontsize=8)
 
  width=width
  height=height
 
  # Draw pdf
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", make.names(prefix), "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.pdf")
  cairo_pdf(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial")
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
 
  # When using R's png function to plot and specifying Arial font, it shows: Windows font database does not have such a font family. How to solve?
  # Answer: Add the following line.
  windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))
  out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", make.names(prefix), "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
  png(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial", units = "in", res=300)
  grid.newpage()
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}
```
```{r}
pan <- c(
"Lcn2",
"Steap4",
"S1pr3",
"Timp1",
"Hspb1",
"Cxcl10",
"Cd44",
"Osmr",
"Cp",
"Serpina3n",
"Aspg",
"Vim",
"Gfap"
)
A1 <- c(
"H2-T23",
"Serping1",
"H2-D1",
"Ggta1",
"ligp1",
"Gbp2",
"Fbln5",
"Ugt1a1",
"Fkbp5",
"Psmb8",
"Srgn",
"Amigo2"
)
A2 <- c(
"Clcf1",
"Tgm1",
"Ptx3",
"S100a10",
"Sphk1",
"Cd109",
"Ptgs2",
"Emp1",
"Slc10a6",
"Tm4sf1",
"B3gnt5",
"Cd14"
)
Immu <- c("Il1b", "Il6", "Tnf", "Ifng", "Tgfb1", "Ccl2", "Ccl3", "Ccl5", "Ccl20", "Cxcl1", "Cxcl10", "C3", "Cfb")
prefix <- "reactive Astro"
ST_DEGs_heatmap(c(pan, A1, A2, Immu), prefix, width=3.68, height=3)
```

#### Glu
```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
# --- 0. Read data and define order (unchanged) ---
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
br.order <- rev(Abeta.merged$brain_region)
br.order <- br.order[!grepl("Ventricle", br.order)]
# --- 1. Prepare data ---
# Assume cell.DEGs and spot.DEGs already exist in the environment
ct <- "Glu"
only_PL_vs_AD <- TRUE
ct_genes <- setdiff(cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster == ct],
                    cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster != ct])
prefix <- "Glu signature"
ST_DEGs_heatmap(ct_genes, prefix, width=3.68, height=11)
```

#### Micro
Differences from Astro:
1. Do not display gene names.
2. border = F
3. Change the chart title to "microglia".
4. Adjust image size.

```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
spot.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
# --- 0. Read data and define order (unchanged) ---
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
br.order <- rev(Abeta.merged$brain_region)
br.order <- br.order[!grepl("Ventricle", br.order)]
# --- 1. Prepare data ---
# Assume cell.DEGs and spot.DEGs already exist in the environment
ct <- "Micro"
only_PL_vs_AD <- TRUE
ct_genes <- setdiff(cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster == ct],
                    cell.DEGs$gene[cell.DEGs$cluster != ct])
sub_dat <- subset(spot.DEGs, gene %in% ct_genes &
                  comparison %in% c("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl", "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl"))
# [Modification 1]: Ensure the brain region list is sorted according to br.order rather than alphabetical order
# This step is critical. Take the intersection first to retain only existing brain regions while preserving the order of br.order
available_regions <- unique(sub_dat$brain_region)
sorted_regions <- br.order[br.order %in% available_regions]
if (only_PL_vs_AD) {
  all_genes <- unique(subset(sub_dat, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")$gene)
} else {
  all_genes <- unique(sub_dat$gene)
}
# --- 2. Matrix extraction function (fine-tuned to adapt to sorting) ---
get_matrix <- function(comp_name) {
  tmp <- dcast(subset(sub_dat, comparison == comp_name),
               gene ~ brain_region, value.var = "avg_log2FC", fill = 0)
  rownames(tmp) <- tmp$gene
  tmp <- tmp[, -1]
 
  # Initialize matrix
  res <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_genes), ncol = length(sorted_regions),
                dimnames = list(all_genes, sorted_regions)) # Note: sorted_regions is used here
 
  common_g <- intersect(rownames(tmp), all_genes)
  common_r <- intersect(colnames(tmp), sorted_regions)
 
  if(length(common_g) > 0 && length(common_r) > 0){
    res[common_g, common_r] <- as.matrix(tmp[common_g, common_r])
  }
 
  # [Core of Modification 1]: Force reordering according to sorted_regions
  res <- res[, sorted_regions, drop=FALSE]
  return(res)
}
mat_AD <- get_matrix("FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl")
mat_PL <- get_matrix("PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
# --- 3. Merge and process column names ---
mat_final <- cbind(mat_AD, mat_PL)
# Force modification of column names to make them unique
colnames(mat_final) <- c(paste0("AD_", sorted_regions), paste0("PL_", sorted_regions))
sorted_regions.renamed <- sub("Olfactory area", "Olfactory cortical area", sorted_regions)
# --- 4. Scale (vectorized method) ---
row_max <- apply(abs(mat_final), 1, max)
# Prevent division by zero
row_max[row_max == 0] <- 1
mat_scaled <- mat_final / row_max
# --- 5. Pre-compute clustering to generate Cluster annotation (Modification 2 & 3) ---
# To obtain specific Cluster numbers (1-6), we need to perform hierarchical clustering before plotting
# Calculate distance and clustering tree
gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled, method = "euclidean")
gene_hclust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "complete")
# Cut genes into 6 clusters
gene_clusters <- cutree(gene_hclust, k = 6)
# Sort the matrix according to clustering results
mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_hclust$order, ]
# Build row annotation data frame (Row Annotation)
# This will add a color bar on the left side of the heatmap with a legend indicating which Cluster
# anno_row <- data.frame(Cluster = factor(paste0("Cluster ", gene_clusters)))
# rownames(anno_row) <- rownames(mat_scaled)
# rescued genes annotation
st.DEGs.rescued <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.rescued.csv", row.names=1)
# In R, I have a dataframe with two columns: gene and direction, both strings. Now I need to group by gene and merge the directions belonging to the same gene into one string separated by commas. Please help me write the code.
# Using dplyr as an example
library(dplyr)
result <- st.DEGs.rescued %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  summarise(direction = paste(unique(direction), collapse = ","))
# Remove contradictions between brain regions.
result2 <- subset(result, direction=="down rescued" | direction=="up rescued")
result2 <- data.frame(result2)
row.names(result2) <- result2$gene
anno_row <- data.frame(Rescued=result2[rownames(mat_scaled), "direction"], row.names=rownames(mat_scaled))
anno_row$Rescued[is.na(anno_row$Rescued)] <- "None"
# --- 6. Plot preparation ---
# Column annotation (Column Annotation)
# anno_col <- data.frame(Group = factor(rep(c("AD vs WT", "PL vs AD"), each = length(sorted_regions)),
# levels = c("AD vs WT", "PL vs AD")))
# rownames(anno_col) <- colnames(mat_scaled)
# Color settings
# Automatically assign colors to 6 Clusters, or you can specify manually
# cluster_colors <- brewer.pal(6, "Set2")
# names(cluster_colors) <- paste0("Cluster ", 1:6)
# anno_colors <- list(
# Group = c("AD vs WT"="#E69F00", "PL vs AD"="#56B4E9"),
# Cluster = cluster_colors # Add color mapping for Cluster
# )
anno_colors <- list(Rescued=c("None"="grey", "up rescued"="red", "down rescued"="blue"))
# Main panel color settings
my_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(100)
# --- 7. Final plotting ---
p <- pheatmap(mat_scaled,
         # Row clustering settings
         # cluster_rows = gene_hclust, # Use the pre-computed clustering tree
         cluster_rows = F,
         # cutree_rows = 6, # [Modification 2]: Divide rows into 6 clusters and add gaps
        
         # Column clustering settings
```
