```{r}
         cluster_cols = FALSE, # [Modification 1]: Turn off column clustering and strictly follow the sorted_regions we set
        
         # Colors and breaks
         color = my_colors, # How to control the length and width of pheatmap legend?
         breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
        
         # Annotations
         # annotation_col = anno_col,
         annotation_row = anno_row, # How to display pheatmap anno row but not show the corresponding legend?
         annotation_legend = FALSE,
         annotation_colors = anno_colors,
        
         # Gap settings
         gaps_col = length(sorted_regions), # Add gap between the two comparison groups
        
         # Label settings
         labels_col = rep(sorted_regions.renamed, 2), # Use original brain region names as labels
         show_rownames = F, # Usually do not show row names when there are too many genes; change to TRUE if needed
        
         main = "Transcriptional alterations of microglia \nsignature genes across comparisons", # I have a heatmap showing scaled log2 fold change of genes highly expressed in astrocytes across two comparison combinations. What should the title of this heatmap be?
         # border_color = NA, # Having borders looks better
         # fontsize=7, # Title font is set below, not bold, to save space.
         fontsize_row=8,
         fontsize_col=8,
         silent = T,
         border_color=NA
)
# Modify font to italic.
library(grid)
idx <- grep("row_names", p$gtable$layout$name)
p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontface = "italic", fontsize=8)
idx <- grep("main", p$gtable$layout$name)
p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]$gp <- gpar(fontsize=8)
width=3.5
height=5
# Draw pdf
out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.pdf")
cairo_pdf(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial")
grid.newpage()
grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()
# When using R's png function to plot and specifying Arial font, it shows: Windows font database does not have such a font family. How to solve?
# Answer: Add the following line.
windowsFonts(Arial = windowsFont("Arial"))
out.path <- paste0("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/", ct, "_ST_DEGs_heatmap.png")
png(out.path, width, height, family = "Arial", units = "in", res=300)
grid.newpage()
grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()
```

#---
## Spot DEGs Classification Discussion: By Specific Pathways
### Investigation
#### Mitochondrial-related genes: MitoCarta3.0
How many genes show significant differential expression?  
Which subdivided pathways do they mainly belong to?

```{r}
mt_db <- readxl::read_excel("../001ST/000data/Mouse.MitoCarta3.0.xls", sheet=2)
spot.DEGs.mt <- subset(spot.DEGs, gene %in% mt_db$Symbol)
length(unique(subset(spot.DEGs.mt, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_WTCtrl")$gene)) # 148
length(unique(subset(spot.DEGs.mt, comparison == "FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl")$gene)) # 106
a <- "OXPHOS > Complex III > CIII subunits | Metabolism > Metals and cofactors > Heme-containing proteins | Metabolism > Electron carriers > Cytochromes | OXPHOS > OXPHOS subunits"
b <- strsplit(a, " | ", fixed = T)
d <- sapply(b[[1]], function(x){
  strsplit(x, " > ", fixed=T)[[1]][1]
})
paste(unique(d), collapse=", ")
```

#### Glycolysis pathway
```{r}
```

#### Comparison with astrocyte subtype genes from other literature
```{r}
st.DEGs <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
astrocytes.markers <- list(GM = c("Gria2", "Slc7a10", "Vegfa", "Fgfr3", "Grin2c"),
Layer1 = c("Apoe", "Clu", "Slc1a2", "Sparcl1", "Aqp4"),
WM = c("Igfbp5", "Ascl1", "Marcks", "Marcksl1", "Fos", "Dbi", "Vim", "Rack1", "Jun", "Thbs4", "Gfap", "Lima1", "S100a6"))
astrocytes.markers2 <- unlist(astrocytes.markers)
df <- data.frame(genes=astrocytes.markers2,
                 subtype=rep(names(astrocytes.markers), sapply(astrocytes.markers, length)),
           is.ST_DEGs=astrocytes.markers2 %in% st.DEGs$gene)
# Try to subtype the Astrocytes genes from different clusters.
df2 <- data.frame(genes=astrocytes.markers2,
                 subtype=rep(names(astrocytes.markers), sapply(astrocytes.markers, length)))
st.DEGs.astro <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox.clustering6/cell_specific/Astro/st.heatmap1.Astro.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.astro2 <- data.frame(genes=row.names(st.DEGs.astro),
                             cluster=st.DEGs.astro$cluster)
st.DEGs.astro3 <- merge(st.DEGs.astro2, df2, by="genes", all.x=T)
row.names(st.DEGs.astro3) <- st.DEGs.astro3$genes
st.DEGs.astro3 <- st.DEGs.astro3[st.DEGs.astro2$genes, ]
# paper: Lipid-accumulated reactive astrocytes promote disease progression in epilepsy
# Fig. 5f
astrocytes.markers <- list("LARA-associated" = c("AQP4", "CST3", "AL627171.2", "CLU", "APOE", "GJA1", "CPE",
                                                 "UBC", "AGT", "GLUL", "TUBB2B", "GAPDH", "ATP1B2", "PTGDS", "CKB",
                                                 "PSAP", "PLCG2", "SCG3", "HTRA1", "WIF1"),
                           "homeostatic" = c("SLC1A3", "GRIA2", "DDX60", "SERPING1", "XAF1", "PER3", "NTM", "VEGFA", "OGT",
                                             "MERTK", "RNF213", "FKBP5", "LENG8"),
                           "reactive" = c("GFAP", "DCLK1", "PSMB8", "HERC6", "STAT1", "GBP3", "USP18", "RTP4", "S100A6"))
# Please help me write an R function named gene_symbol_human_to_mouse that converts human gene symbols to mouse gene symbols. The input is a vector containing multiple human gene symbols. Human gene symbols are all uppercase letters, while mouse gene symbols have only the first letter capitalized.
gene_symbol_human_to_mouse <- function(human_genes) {
  # Check if input is a character vector
  if (!is.character(human_genes)) {
    stop("Input must be a character vector")
  }
 
  # Create result vector
  mouse_genes <- character(length(human_genes))
 
  # Process each gene
  for (i in seq_along(human_genes)) {
    gene <- human_genes[i]
   
    # Skip empty values or NA
    if (is.na(gene) || gene == "") {
      mouse_genes[i] <- NA
      next
    }
   
    # Special gene conversion rules (only some common examples are listed here)
    special_cases <- list(
      "TP53" = "Trp53",
      "MYC" = "Myc",
      "EGFR" = "Egfr",
      "KRAS" = "Kras",
      "BRAF" = "Braf",
      "BRCA1" = "Brca1",
      "BRCA2" = "Brca2",
      "AKT1" = "Akt1",
      "PIK3CA" = "Pik3ca",
      "PTEN" = "Pten"
      # You can add more special conversion rules here
    )
   
    # Check if it is a special gene
    if (gene %in% names(special_cases)) {
      mouse_genes[i] <- special_cases[[gene]]
    } else {
      # General conversion rule: capitalize the first letter, lowercase the rest
      mouse_genes[i] <- paste0(
        toupper(substr(gene, 1, 1)),
        tolower(substr(gene, 2, nchar(gene)))
      )
    }
  }
 
  return(mouse_genes)
}
astrocytes.markers.mouse <- lapply(astrocytes.markers, gene_symbol_human_to_mouse)
```

#### Comparison with calcium overload-related genes
```{r}
st.DEGs <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
gene.calcium <- readLines("../004explore/excito/calcium.list")
st.DEGs.sub <- subset(st.DEGs, st.DEGs$gene %in% gene.calcium)
subset(st.DEGs.sub, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region
# Hspa51 1.908734e-37 -0.2671808 0.916 0.965 3.540893e-33 Isocortex
# Hspa53 8.251181e-13 -0.2577938 0.941 0.956 1.530677e-08 Hypothalamus
# Hspa54 3.045602e-14 -0.3270697 0.599 0.725 5.649897e-10 Fiber tracts
# Hspa56 3.733166e-12 -0.2520610 0.968 0.967 6.925397e-08 Cortical subplate
# Hspa58 1.238383e-28 -0.3241217 0.847 0.953 2.297324e-24 Thalamus
# Jun2 6.885083e-15 -0.2166024 0.386 0.590 1.277252e-10 Thalamus
# Xbp12 4.505552e-07 -0.1359585 0.509 0.651 8.358249e-03 Thalamus
# Hspa511 3.501544e-24 -0.3309577 0.870 0.950 6.495715e-20 Olfactory area
# Hspa514 2.419873e-24 -0.3380465 0.886 0.974 4.489107e-20 Hippocampus
# comparison gene
# Hspa51 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa53 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa54 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa56 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa58 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Jun2 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Jun
# Xbp12 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Xbp1
# Hspa511 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa514 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Hspa5
# Hspa5, Jun, and Xbp1 are all downregulated in the thalamus.
# Hspa5: ER stress (UPR), molecular chaperone (IRE1-XBP1 pathway)
# Jun: CaMK-CREB pathway, pro-apoptotic (JNK pathway)
# Xbp1: ER stress (UPR), activates ER stress adaptive genes after splicing
subset(st.DEGs.sub, comparison == "FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl" & gene %in% c("Hspa5", "Jun", "Xbp1"))
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region
# Hspa5 2.714780e-10 -0.1464914 0.965 0.966 5.036188e-06 Isocortex
# Xbp1 7.073470e-07 -0.1027323 0.788 0.815 1.312200e-02 Isocortex
# Jun 4.828877e-08 -0.2414346 0.424 0.627 8.958050e-04 Cortical subplate
# Jun3 1.116605e-07 -0.1529165 0.568 0.722 2.071414e-03 Olfactory area
# Hspa510 1.351760e-06 -0.1198292 0.950 0.984 2.507649e-02 Olfactory area
# comparison gene
# Hspa5 FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Hspa5
# Xbp1 FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Xbp1
# Jun FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Jun
# Jun3 FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Jun
# Hspa510 FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl Hspa5
# Although downregulated in PL vs AD, these genes are also downregulated in AD vs WT.
```

#### Comparison with mitochondrial dysfunction-related genes
```{r}
st.DEGs <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
gene.list <- readLines("../004explore/oxphos/mito_dysf.list")
st.DEGs.sub <- subset(st.DEGs, st.DEGs$gene %in% gene.list)
subset(st.DEGs.sub, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region
# Ldha 2.111922e-14 -0.1450687 0.986 0.991 3.917826e-10 Isocortex
# Ldha3 2.960633e-28 -0.3211464 0.986 0.999 5.492270e-24 Olfactory area
# Vdac1 3.028440e-07 -0.1564624 0.977 0.982 5.618060e-03 Olfactory area
# Ldha5 8.308486e-07 -0.2507557 0.982 1.000 1.541307e-02 Striatum
# comparison gene
# Ldha PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Ldha
# Ldha3 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Ldha
# Vdac1 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Vdac1
# Ldha5 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl Ldha
# The direction is inconsistent with expectations.
# Ldha: Metabolic reprogramming (glycolysis), increased lactate production (compensatory energy supply)
# Vdac1: ER-mitochondria coupling, increased mitochondrial membrane permeability (increased calcium release)
```

#### Gene List Organization
- Retain AD dysregulated genes
- AD risk genes
- Inflammation-related genes
- Marker genes of different cell types in SC
- PIGs

The gene list should be similar to that of SC. You can add marker genes of different cell types from SC.

**Organize interested_genes:**
```{r eval=FALSE}
# # Inflammation-related, AD risk genes: 001Seurat_out/004DEGs/interested_genes.csv
# Marker genes: 002SC/001Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/cluster.L2/cluster_markers.csv
interested_genes <- read.csv("../002SC/001Seurat_out/004DEGs/interested_genes.csv", row.names=1)
interested_pathways <- c("Aggregation",
                         "Processing / trafficking",
                         "Clearance / degradation",
                         "Mediator of Abeta toxicity",
                         "inflammatory_response")
interested_genes <- interested_genes[interested_genes$pathway %in% interested_pathways, ]
cluster_markers_genes <- read.csv("../002SC/001Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/cluster.L2/cluster_markers.csv", row.names=1)
table(cluster_markers_genes$cluster)
# Take top20, or filter by p_val_adj < 0.05 & avg_log2FC > 1.
cluster_markers_genes.sub <- subset(cluster_markers_genes, p_val_adj < 0.05 & avg_log2FC > 1)
table(cluster_markers_genes.sub$cluster)
cluster_markers_genes.sub <- cluster_markers_genes.sub[, c("gene", "cluster")]
colnames(cluster_markers_genes.sub) <- c("gene", "pathway")
interested_genes.df <- rbind(interested_genes, cluster_markers_genes.sub)
```

#### Intersection Analysis Between DEGs and Gene Lists
Perform hypergeometric tests between the organized gene lists and each DEGs list respectively.

```{r eval=FALSE}
#' is_top_n
#'
#' @param v A vector containing duplicate items.
#' @param n For each duplicate item, retain the top n items.
#'
#' @return A logical vector, TRUE for items to retain, FALSE for items not to retain.
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
is_top_n <- function(v, n=3){
  # Vectorized style: two steps, 1) mark rank first, 2) then judge whether it is top_n.
  # Boundary issues are simplified and less prone to errors.
  # 1) Mark rank first
  mark <- 1
  v.rank <- c(mark)
  last <- v[1]
  for (i in 2:length(v)){
    if (v[i] == last) {
      mark <- mark + 1
    } else {
      mark <- 1
      last <- v[i]
    }
    v.rank[i] <- mark
  }
  # 2) Then judge whether it is top_n
  v.is_top <- v.rank <= n
  return(v.is_top)
}
#' to_multi_gene_list
#'
#' @param df
#' @param group.col
#' @param gene.col
#' @param sep
#' @param top_n For each group, only take the top n genes. Requires df to be sorted.
#'
#' @return
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
#' df1 <- to_multi_gene_list(cluster_markers, top_n=6)
to_multi_gene_list <- function(df,
                               group.col="cluster",
                               gene.col="gene",
                               sep=";",
                               top_n=NULL){
  df <- df[, c(group.col, gene.col)]
  if (! is.null(top_n)){
    df <- df[is_top_n(df[[group.col]], n=top_n), ]
  }
  colnames(df) <- c("group", "gene")
  df <- aggregate(gene~group,
                  FUN=function(x){paste(x, collapse=sep)},
                  data=df)
  colnames(df) <- c(group.col, gene.col)
  return(df)
}
from_multi_gene_list <- function(df, group.col="cluster",
                                 gene.col="gene", sep=";"){
  df <- df[df[[gene.col]] != "", ]
  df.list <- lapply(1:nrow(df), function(x){
    gene.list <- df[x, gene.col]
    gene.list <- strsplit(gene.list, sep)[[1]]
    df1 <- cbind.data.frame(group=df[x, group.col], gene=gene.list)
    return(df1)
  })
  out.df <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, df.list)
  colnames(out.df) <- c(group.col, gene.col)
  return(out.df)
}
#' batchPhyper2
#'
#' Investigate whether DEGs lists significantly overlap with each interested pathway.
#'
#' @param df1 Contains two columns: listName and geneList.
#' @param df2 Contains two columns: gene and pathway.
#' @param PopSize background gene number
#'
#' @return
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
batchPhyper2 <- function(df1, df2,
                         group.col.1="listName",
                         group.col.2="pathway",
                         gene.col.1="geneList",
                         gene.col.2="gene",
                         show.genes=T,
                         PopSize=20000){
  clusters <- unique(df1[[group.col.1]])
  names(clusters) <- clusters
  out <- lapply(clusters, function(cluster){
    marker_sets <- unique(df2[[group.col.2]])
    names(marker_sets) <- marker_sets
    sig.mark <- sapply(marker_sets, function(cell_type){
      s1 <- df1[df1[[group.col.1]]==cluster, gene.col.1, drop=T]
      s2 <- df2[df2[[group.col.2]]==cell_type, gene.col.2, drop=T]
      overlap.genes <- intersect(s1, s2)
      overlap <- length(overlap.genes)
      list1 <- length(s1)
      list2 <- length(s2)
      p <- phyper(overlap-1,
                  list1,
                  PopSize-list1,
                  list2,
                  lower.tail = FALSE,
                  log.p = FALSE)
      p <- ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", "-")
      cell.content <- paste0("(", p, ") ", overlap)
      if (show.genes & length(overlap.genes) > 0) {
        cell.content <- paste0(cell.content, ": ",
                             paste(overlap.genes, collapse=";"))
      }
      return(cell.content)
    })
    return(sig.mark)
  })
  return(do.call(rbind, out))
}
# all DEGs
for (log2fc in c(0.3)){
  in.file <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                    log2fc, ".csv")
  DEGs <- read.csv(in.file, row.names=1)
  DEGs2 <- data.frame(listName=paste0(DEGs$comparison, ".",
                                     DEGs$cellType, ".",
                                     DEGs$direction),
                     geneList=DEGs$geneList)
 
  DEGs2 <- from_multi_gene_list(DEGs2, group.col="listName",
                                   gene.col="geneList")
 
 
  DEGs.tested <- batchPhyper2(DEGs2, interested_genes.df)
  out.file <- paste0("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_",
                     log2fc, ".hyper_tested.csv")
  write.csv(DEGs.tested, out.file)
}
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.hyper_tested.csv](data/st/003/wilcox.each_cell.log2fc_0.3.hyper_tested.csv): Hypergeometric test results between each DEGs list and the pathways of interest.

## Other Analyses
### Intersection with DEGs from ROSMAP and MSBB
【Analysis】 Find the similarities and differences between DEGs lists identified in SC and ST and DEGs lists identified in ROSMAP and MSBB.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Original script: ../check_SC_DEGs.r
get_unique_genes <- function(x){
  y <- paste(x, collapse=";")
  y <- strsplit(y, ";")[[1]]
  y <- setdiff(y, "")
  return(unique(y))
}
count_unique_genes <- function(x){
  y <- get_unique_genes(x)
  return(length(y))
}
has_gene <- function(x, g){
  y <- strsplit(x, ";")[[1]]
  return (g %in% y)
}
test_intersect <- function(set1, set2, PopSize=20000){
  out <- intersect(set1, set2)
  overlap <- length(out)
  list1 <- length(set1)
  list2 <- length(set2)
  p <- phyper(overlap-1, list1, PopSize-list1, list2, lower.tail = FALSE, log.p = FALSE)
  return(p)
}
data <- read.csv("003two_seq_type/001DEG/DEG.final.csv", row.names=1)
# Compare the difference in total number of up/down DEGs between SC and ST.
data2 <- subset(data, method=="wilcoxon" & cellType != "all")
# SC has more cell types and may list many DEGs repeatedly, so duplicates must be removed for comparison.
out.wilcox <- aggregate(geneList~seqType+comparison, FUN=count_unique_genes, data=data2)
data3 <- subset(data, method=="DESeq2" & cellType != "all")
out.deseq2 <- aggregate(geneList~seqType+comparison, FUN=count_unique_genes, data=data3)
# find overlap: Intersection of DEGs between SC and ST.
for (M in c("wilcoxon", "DESeq2")){
  for(D in c("up", "down")){
    drug_effect.st <- subset(data, comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" &
                                         method == M &
                                         seqType=="ST" & direction==D,
                                       select="geneList", drop=T)
    drug_effect.sc <- subset(data, comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" &
                                         method == M &
                                         seqType=="SC" & direction==D,
                                       select="geneList", drop=T)
    drug_effect.st <- get_unique_genes(drug_effect.st)
    drug_effect.sc <- get_unique_genes(drug_effect.sc)
    out <- intersect(drug_effect.st, drug_effect.sc)
    overlap <- length(out)
    list1 <- length(drug_effect.st)
    list2 <- length(drug_effect.sc)
    PopSize <- 20000
    p <- phyper(overlap-1,list1,PopSize-list1,list2,lower.tail = FALSE, log.p = FALSE)
    cat("method: ", M, ";", "direction: ", D, "; pvalue: ", p, "\n")
    print(out)
    print("----------------")
  }
}
search_gene <- function(g){
  data[sapply(data$geneList, has_gene, g=g), 1:5]
}
search_gene("Apoe")
# Find similarities and differences between DEGs lists identified in SC/ST and DEGs lists identified in ROSMAP/MSBB.
library(homologene)
# 246 rows, including duplicate genes. After removing duplicates, there are 149 genes. Among them, 110 genes can find homologous genes in mouse.
rosmap.deg <- read.csv("000data/ROSMAP.DEGs.fdr_0.05_fc_1.2.tsv", sep="\t")
homolo <- homologene(rosmap.deg$geneSymbol, inTax = 9606, outTax = 10090)
length(unique(homolo$`9606`)) # 110
homolo <- data.frame(geneSymbol=homolo$`9606`, geneSymbol.mouse=homolo$`10090`)
rosmap.deg <- merge(rosmap.deg, homolo, by="geneSymbol")
rosmap.AD_vs_NL.up <- unique(rosmap.deg[rosmap.deg$logFC > 0, "geneSymbol.mouse"])
rosmap.AD_vs_NL.down <- unique(rosmap.deg[rosmap.deg$logFC < 0, "geneSymbol.mouse"])
# find overlap: Intersection between DEGs identified in this study and DEGs identified in rosmap/msbb.
out.list <- list()
n <- 1
for (M in c("wilcoxon", "DESeq2")){
  for(D in c("up", "down")){
    for (SeqType in c("ST", "SC")){
      drug_effect <- subset(data, comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" &
                                           method == M &
                                           seqType==SeqType & direction==D,
                                         select="geneList", drop=T)
      drug_effect <- get_unique_genes(drug_effect)
      if (D== "up") {
        rosmap.set <- rosmap.AD_vs_NL.down
      } else {
        rosmap.set <- rosmap.AD_vs_NL.up
      }
      s <- intersect(drug_effect, rosmap.set)
      p <- test_intersect(drug_effect, rosmap.set)
      cat("SeqType: ", SeqType, "; method: ", M, "; direction: ", D, "; pvalue: ", p, "\n")
      cat("geneList: ", s, "\n")
      cat("----------------\n\n")
      out.list[[n]] <- c(SeqType=SeqType, method=M, direction=D, pvalue=p, geneList=paste(s, collapse=";"))
      n <- n + 1
    }
  }
}
out.df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, out.list))
write.csv(out.df, "003two_seq_type/001DEG/intersect_with_rosmap.csv")
msbb.deg <- read.csv("000data/lmfit.DEGs.fdr_0.05_fc_1.2.tsv", sep="\t")
homolo <- homologene(msbb.deg$Symbol, inTax = 9606, outTax = 10090)
length(unique(msbb.deg$Symbol)) # 8725
length(unique(homolo$`9606`)) # 4974
homolo <- data.frame(Symbol=homolo$`9606`, geneSymbol.mouse=homolo$`10090`)
msbb.deg <- merge(msbb.deg, homolo, by="Symbol")
msbb.up <- unique(msbb.deg[msbb.deg$logFC > 0, "geneSymbol.mouse"])
msbb.down <- unique(msbb.deg[msbb.deg$logFC < 0, "geneSymbol.mouse"])
out.list <- list()
n <- 1
for (M in c("wilcoxon", "DESeq2")){
  for(D in c("up", "down")){
    for (SeqType in c("ST", "SC")){
      drug_effect <- subset(data, comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl" &
                                           method == M &
                                           seqType==SeqType & direction==D,
                                         select="geneList", drop=T)
      drug_effect <- get_unique_genes(drug_effect)
      if (D== "up") {
        gset <- msbb.down
      } else {
        gset <- msbb.up
      }
      s <- intersect(drug_effect, gset)
      p <- test_intersect(drug_effect, gset)
      cat("SeqType: ", SeqType, "; method: ", M, "; direction: ", D, "; pvalue: ", p, "\n")
      cat("geneList: ", s, "\n")
      cat("----------------\n\n")
      out.list[[n]] <- c(SeqType=SeqType, method=M, direction=D,
                         pvalue=p, nGenes=length(s), geneList=paste(s, collapse=";"))
      n <- n + 1
    }
  }
}
out.df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, out.list))
write.csv(out.df, "003two_seq_type/001DEG/intersect_with_msbb.csv")
```

### Microglia Depletion Rate in Each Brain Region in Spatial Transcriptomics
```{r}
st.DEGs <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/003DEGs/wilcox/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
micro.marker <- "C1qa"
df <- subset(st.DEGs, gene == micro.marker & comparison=="PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
df$depletion_rate <- 1 - 2^df$avg_log2FC
mean(df$depletion_rate)
# C1qa: 0.4016909
# C1qb: 0.3956579
# C1qc: 0.3615615
# > df
# p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj brain_region
# C1qa1 1.199030e-75 -0.5842970 0.662 0.864 2.224321e-71 Isocortex
# C1qa3 3.097635e-46 -0.6263182 0.423 0.798 5.746423e-42 Hypothalamus
# C1qa5 7.498844e-19 -0.8805762 0.497 0.721 1.391110e-14 Meninges
# C1qa8 1.144687e-56 -0.9656266 0.448 0.774 2.123509e-52 Fiber tracts
# C1qa10 1.455870e-23 -0.5205228 0.550 0.748 2.700785e-19 Cortical subplate
# C1qa12 1.238540e-107 -0.9845902 0.685 0.970 2.297616e-103 Thalamus
# C1qa15 9.939536e-07 -1.1480681 0.456 0.770 1.843883e-02 Lateral Ventricle
# C1qa16 8.669477e-10 -0.6653558 0.338 0.800 1.608275e-05 Third Ventricle
# C1qa18 1.401610e-48 -0.6198844 0.670 0.901 2.600127e-44 Olfactory area
# C1qa20 1.603619e-17 -0.6005125 0.689 0.921 2.974874e-13 Striatum
# C1qa22 7.141997e-49 -0.6968952 0.627 0.942 1.324912e-44 Hippocampus
# comparison gene depletion_rate
# C1qa1 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3330258
# C1qa3 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3521724
# C1qa5 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.4568495
# C1qa8 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.4879440
# C1qa10 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3028808
# C1qa12 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.4946307
# C1qa15 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.5487709
# C1qa16 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3694668
# C1qa18 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3492769
# C1qa20 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3404804
# C1qa22 PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl C1qa 0.3831016
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Do not use log2fc from FindMarkers.
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(gobj)
st.seurat$treatment_ct <- paste0(st.seurat$treatment, "_", st.seurat$cell_types)
 
Idents(st.seurat) <- "treatment_ct"
meta <- st.seurat@meta.data
celltypes <- setdiff(unique(st.seurat$cell_types), "C12")
qry <- "PLX5622"
ref <- "FADCtrl"
df.lfc.list <- list()
for (br in celltypes) {
  cells.1 <- row.names(meta)[meta$treatment_ct == paste0(qry, "_", br)]
  cells.2 <- row.names(meta)[meta$treatment_ct == paste0(ref, "_", br)]
  df.fc <- FoldChange(st.seurat@assays$RNA,
                      cells.1=cells.1,
                      cells.2=cells.2,
                      features=c("C1qa", "Aif1", "Tmem119", "P2ry12", "Tyrobp", "Trem2"),
                      norm.method="LogNormalize",
                      pseudocount.use = 0)
  df.fc$brain_region <- br
  df.fc$gene <- row.names(df.fc)
  df.lfc.list[[br]] <- df.fc
}
df.lfc <- Reduce(rbind, df.lfc.list)
df.lfc$depletion_rate <- 1 - 2^df.lfc$avg_log2FC
mean(df.lfc[df.lfc$gene == "C1qa", "depletion_rate"])
# [1] 0.6161806
sd(df.lfc[df.lfc$gene == "C1qa", "depletion_rate"])
# [1] 0.08196494
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc[df.lfc$gene == "C1qa", ]
df.lfc.sub[order(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate), ]
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc[df.lfc$gene %in% c("Tmem119", "P2ry12"), ]
mean(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate) # 0.7286764
sd(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate) # 0.09242443
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc[df.lfc$gene %in% c("Tyrobp", "Trem2"), ]
mean(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate) # 0.5414908
sd(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate) # 0.0949244
library(ggplot2)
p <- ggplot(df.lfc, aes(brain_region, depletion_rate, fill=gene)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip()
ggsave("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/micro_depletion_rate.jpg", p)
# Only plot C1qa
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc[df.lfc$gene == "C1qa", ]
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc.sub[order(df.lfc.sub$depletion_rate), ]
df.lfc.sub$brain_region <- factor(df.lfc.sub$brain_region, levels=df.lfc.sub$brain_region, ordered=T)
p <- ggplot(df.lfc.sub, aes(brain_region, depletion_rate)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  coord_flip()
ggsave("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/micro_depletion_rate.C1qa.jpg", p)
# Plot DAM and HOM
df.lfc.sub <- df.lfc[df.lfc$gene %in% c("Trem2", "Tmem119"), ]
p <- ggplot(df.lfc.sub, aes(brain_region, depletion_rate, fill=gene)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip()
ggsave("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/micro_depletion_rate.DAM_HOM.jpg", p)
```

### Proportion of Microglia in Each Brain Region in Spatial Transcriptomics
```{r}
# Comparison of C1qa expression across different brain regions
meta$C1qa <- as.vector(st.seurat@assays$RNA@data["C1qa", ])
df <- aggregate(C1qa~treatment+cell_types, FUN=function(x){mean(expm1(x))}, data=meta)
p <- ggplot(df, aes(cell_types, C1qa)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  coord_flip() + facet_grid(.~treatment)
ggsave("001Giotto_out/003DEGs/micro_br_expression.jpg", p, width=7, height=4)
```

### Differences in Depletion Rate Between Spots Far from and Close to Aβ (by Gene)
```{r}
# Do not use log2fc from FindMarkers.
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(gobj)
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)
all(meta$cell_ID == st.seurat$cell_ID)
st.seurat$Abeta_index <- meta$Abeta_index
st.seurat$P2ry12 <- meta$P2ry12
st.seurat$Gfap <- meta$Gfap
st.seurat$Abeta_HL <- ifelse(meta$Abeta_index > 10, "H", "L")
st.seurat$treatment_ct <- paste0(st.seurat$treatment, "_", st.seurat$cell_types)
 
Idents(st.seurat) <- "treatment_ct"
celltypes <- setdiff(unique(st.seurat$cell_types), "C12")
qry <- "PLX5622"
ref <- "FADCtrl"
df.lfc.list <- list()
for (br in celltypes) {
  for (HL in c("H", "L")) {
    cells.1 <- row.names(st.seurat@meta.data)[st.seurat$treatment_ct == paste0(qry, "_", br) & st.seurat$Abeta_HL == HL]
    cells.2 <- row.names(st.seurat@meta.data)[st.seurat$treatment_ct == paste0(ref, "_", br) & st.seurat$Abeta_HL == HL]
    df.fc <- FoldChange(st.seurat@assays$RNA,
                        cells.1=cells.1,
                        cells.2=cells.2,
                        features=c("C1qa", "Aif1", "Tmem119", "P2ry12", "Tyrobp", "Trem2", "Myo1e", "Igf1", "Fgf13", "Plxdc2", "Ctsd"),
                        norm.method="LogNormalize",
                        pseudocount.use = 0)
    df.fc$brain_region <- br
    df.fc$gene <- row.names(df.fc)
    df.fc$Abeta_HL <- HL
    df.lfc.list[[paste0(br, ".", HL)]] <- df.fc
  }
}
df.lfc <- Reduce(rbind, df.lfc.list)
df.lfc$depletion_rate <- 1 - 2^df.lfc$avg_log2FC
write.csv(df.lfc, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/Abeta_HL_region_depletion_rate.csv")
```

### Differences in Depletion Rate Between Spots Far from and Close to Aβ (by Deconvolution)
```{r}
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(gobj)
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)
df.deconvo <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/cell2location/level2/spot.anno.prop.csv", row.names=1)
row.names(df.deconvo) <- df.deconvo$cell.id
df.deconvo <- df.deconvo[meta$cell_ID, ]
all(meta$cell_ID == st.seurat$cell_ID)
st.seurat$Abeta_index <- meta$Abeta_index
st.seurat$Abeta_HL <- ifelse(meta$Abeta_index > 10, "H", "L")
st.seurat$Micro_0_1 <- df.deconvo$Micro_0_1
st.seurat$treatment_ct <- paste0(st.seurat$treatment, "_", st.seurat$cell_types)
 
Idents(st.seurat) <- "treatment_ct"
out.df <- aggregate(Micro_0_1 ~ treatment+cell_types+Abeta_HL, FUN=mean, data=st.seurat@meta.data)
out.df.sub <- subset(out.df, treatment != "WTCtrl")
library(reshape2)
out.df.sub <- dcast(cell_types+Abeta_HL ~ treatment, value.var="Micro_0_1", data=out.df.sub)
out.df.sub$depletion_rate <- (out.df.sub$FADCtrl - out.df.sub$PLX5622) / out.df.sub$FADCtrl
write.csv(out.df.sub, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/Abeta_HL_region_depletion_rate.deconvo.csv")
```

### Differences Between Spots Far from and Close to Aβ
1. Load data. ok  
2. Subset specific brain region.  
3. Classify according to Aβ plaque area.  
4. Differential expression analysis.

```{r}
setwd("/home/mouse_ad/001ST")
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
st.seurat <- Giotto2Seurat(gobj)
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)
all(meta$cell_ID == st.seurat$cell_ID)
st.seurat$Abeta_index <- meta$Abeta_index
st.seurat$P2ry12 <- meta$P2ry12
st.seurat$Gfap <- meta$Gfap
Idents(st.seurat) <- "cell_types"
st.seurat.sub <- subset(st.seurat, ident="Olfactory area")
```
